Check auto-approve rules from exceptions.yaml\n130\t    2. Either execute directly (auto-approved) OR create bead (manual approval)\n131\t    3. Return pending-card spec (if bead created) or execution result\n132\t    \"\"\"\n133\t\n134\t    def __init__(self, store=None):\n135\t        self.store = store\n136\t        self._zai_client = None\n137\t        self._reload_manager = None\n138\t\n139\t    async def _get_zai_client(self):\n140\t        \"\"\"Get or create ZAI client.\"\"\"\n141\t        if self._zai_client is None:\n142\t            self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n143\t        return self._zai_client\n144\t\n145\t    async def _get_store(self):\n146\t        \"\"\"Get or create session store.\"\"\"\n147\t        if self.store is None:\n148\t            self.store = get_store()\n149\t        return self.store\n150\t\n151\t    def _get_reload_manager(self):\n152\t        \"\"\"Get or create hot-reload manager.\"\"\"\n153\t        if self._reload_manager is None:\n154\t            self._reload_manager = get_reload_manager()\n155\t        return self._reload_manager\n156\t\n157\t    def _evaluate_auto_approve(self, request: EscalateRequest, exceptions_config: dict) -> tuple[bool, str | None]:\n158\t        \"\"\"\n159\t        Evaluate if an intent should be auto-approved based on exceptions.yaml.\n160\t\n161\t        Args:\n162\t            request: The escalate request\n163\t            exceptions_config: The loaded exceptions.yaml config\n164\t\n165\t        Returns:\n166\t            (auto_approve: bool, reason: str | None)\n167\t        \"\"\"\n168\t        auto_approve_rules = exceptions_config.get(\"auto_approve\", {})\n169\t\n170\t        # Extract context for rule evaluation\n171\t        context = {\n172\t            \"environment\": request.metadata.get(\"environment\"),\n173\t            \"project_slug\": request.project_slug,\n174\t            \"branch\": request.metadata.get(\"branch\"),\n175\t            \"action\": request.metadata.get(\"action\"),\n176\t            \"risk_level\": request.metadata.get(\"risk_level\", \"normal\"),\n177\t        }\n178\t\n179\t        # Check read-only operations (always auto-approved)\n180\t        read_only_actions = auto_approve_rules.get(\"read_only\", [])\n181\t        action = context.get(\"action\")\n182\t        if action in read_only_actions:\n183\t            return True, f\"Read-only operation: {action}\"\n184\t\n185\t        # Check safe mutations with conditions\n186\t        safe_mutations = auto_approve_rules.get(\"safe_mutations\", [])\n187\t        for rule in safe_mutations:\n188\t            condition = rule.get(\"condition\", \"\")\n189\t            actions = rule.get(\"actions\", [])\n190\t\n191\t            # Simple condition evaluation (supports basic comparisons)\n192\t            if self._evaluate_condition(condition, context) and action in actions:\n193\t                return True, f\"Safe mutation in {condition}: {action}\"\n194\t\n195\t        # Check manual approval rules (never auto-approve)\n196\t        manual_rules = exceptions_config.get(\"manual_approval\", [])\n197\t        for rule in manual_rules:\n198\t            condition = rule.get(\"condition\", \"\")\n199\t            actions = rule.get(\"actions\", [])\n200\t\n201\t            # Check if the action is in this rule's actions\n202\t            if action not in actions:\n203\t                continue\n204\t\n205\t            # Evaluate the condition\n206\t            if self._evaluate_condition(condition, context):\n207\t                if rule.get(\"always_approve\") is False:\n208\t                    return False, f\"Manual approval required: {condition}\"\n209\t\n210\t        # Check approval workflow rules\n211\t        approval_config = exceptions_config.get(\"approval\", {})\n212\t        never_auto_approve = approval_config.get(\"never_auto_approve\", [])\n213\t        for condition_str in never_auto_approve:\n214\t            if self._evaluate_condition(condition_str, context):\n215\t                return False, f\"Never auto-approve: {condition_str}\"\n216\t\n217\t        # Default: require manual approval for unknown actions\n218\t        return False, \"Unknown action - requires manual approval\"\n219\t\n220\t    def _evaluate_condition(self, condition: str, context: dict) -> bool:\n221\t        \"\"\"\n222\t        Evaluate a condition string against context.\n223\t\n224\t        Supports simple conditions like:\n225\t        - \"environment == 'staging'\"\n226\t        - \"branch == 'main' || branch == 'master'\"  (using || for OR)\n227\t        - \"action == 'kubectl_delete_namespace'\"\n228\t        - \"environment == 'staging' && action == 'restart'\"  (using && for AND)\n229\t\n230\t        Note: Supports both shell-style operators (||, &&) and Python-style (or, and).\n231\t\n232\t        Args:\n233\t            condition: The condition string\n234\t            context: Dict of available variables\n235\t\n236\t        Returns:\n237\t            True if condition evaluates to True, False otherwise\n238\t        \"\"\"\n239\t        try:\n240\t            # Translate shell-style operators to Python syntax\n241\t            # (People are more familiar with || and && from bash/js)\n242\t            python_condition = condition.replace(\" || \", \" or \").replace(\" && \", \" and \")\n243\t\n244\t            # Create a safe evaluation environment\n245\t            safe_globals = {\"__builtins__\": {}}\n246\t            safe_locals = {\n247\t                \"environment\": context.get(\"environment\"),\n248\t                \"project_slug\": context.get(\"project_slug\"),\n249\t                \"branch\": context.get(\"branch\"),\n250\t                \"action\": context.get(\"action\"),\n251\t                \"risk_level\": context.get(\"risk_level\"),\n252\t            }\n253\t\n254\t            # Evaluate condition\n255\t            result = eval(python_condition, safe_globals, safe_locals)\n256\t            return bool(result)\n257\t        except Exception as e:\n258\t            logger.warning(f\"Failed to evaluate condition '{condition}': {e}\")\n259\t            return False\n260\t\n261\t    def _get_bead_type_from_targets(self, intent_type: str, exceptions_config: dict) -> str:\n262\t        \"\"\"\n263\t        Get the bead type from escalation_targets config.\n264\t\n265\t        Args:\n266\t            intent_type: The intent type from IntentRouter (e.g., 'action', 'task-profile', 'self-modification')\n267\t            exceptions_config: The loaded exceptions.yaml config\n268\t\n269\t        Returns:\n270\t            The bead type to create (default: 'task')\n271\t        \"\"\"\n272\t        # Map IntentRouter types to escalation_targets keys\n273\t        intent_type_mapping = {\n274\t            \"action\": \"action\",\n275\t            \"task-profile\": \"action\",  # Task-profile intents create action-type beads\n276\t            \"self-modification\": \"self_modification\",\n277\t            \"monitoring-config\": \"monitoring_config\",\n278\t        }\n279\t\n280\t        # Route to appropriate executor based on action type\n305\t            if action == \"kubectl_delete_pod\":\n306\t                return await self._execute_delete_pod(request)\n307\t            elif action.startswith(\"kubectl_\"):\n308\t                return await self._execute_kubectl_command(request)\n309\t            elif action.startswith(\"git_\"):\n310\t                return await self._execute_git_command(request)\n311\t            else:\n312\t                # Unknown action - return placeholder for now\n313\t                logger.warning(f\"Unknown auto-approved action: {action}\")\n314\t                return {\n315\t                    \"status\": \"pending\",\n316\t                    \"summary\": f\"Auto-approved action '{action}' not yet implemented\",\n317\t                    \"data\": {\n318\t                        \"action\": action,\n319\t                        \"utterance\": request.utterance,\n320\t                    },\n321\t                    \"urgency\": \"low\",\n322\t                }\n323\t\n324\t        except CommandExecutionError as e:\n325\t            logger.error(f\"Command execution failed for intent {request.intent_id}: {e}\")\n326\t            return {\n327\t                \"status\": \"failed\",\n328\t                \"summary\": f\"Command execution failed: {str(e)}\",\n329\t                \"data\": {\n330\t                    \"action\": action,\n331\t                    \"error\": str(e),\n332\t                },\n333\t                \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n334\t            }\n335\t        except Exception as e:\n336\t            logger.error(f\"Unexpected error executing auto-approved action: {e}\")\n337\t            return {\n338\t                \"status\": \"failed\",\n339\t                \"summary\": f\"Unexpected error: {str(e)}\",\n340\t                \"data\": {\n341\t                    \"action\": action,\n342\t                    \"error\": str(e),\n343\t                },\n344\t                \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n345\t            }\n346\t\n347\t    async def _execute_delete_pod(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> dict:\n348\t        \"\"\"\n349\t        Execute kubectl delete pod command.\n350\t\n351\t        Args:\n352\t            request: The escalate request\n353\t\n354\t        Returns:\n355\t            Execution result dict\n356\t        \"\"\"\n357\t        executor = get_kubectl_executor()\n358\t\n359\t        # Build context for the LLM\n431\t        context_parts = [\n432\t            f\"User utterance: {request.utterance}\",\n433\t            f\"Intent type: {request.intent_type}\",\n434\t        ]\n435\t\n436\t        if request.project_slug:\n437\t            context_parts.append(f\"Project: {request.project_slug}\")\n438\t\n439\t        if request.topic_id:\n440\t            context_parts.append(f\"Topic ID: {request.topic_id}\")\n441\t\n442\t        # Add any additional context\n443\t        if request.context:\n444\t            context_parts.append(f\"\\nAdditional context:\")\n445\t            for key, value in request.context.items():\n446\t                context_parts.append(f\"  {key}: {value}\")\n447\t\n448\t        # Build the user message\n449\t        user_message = \"\\n\".join([\n450\t            \"Formulate a NEEDLE bead body for this intent:\",\n451\t            \"\",\n452\t            *context_parts,\n453\t            \"\",\n454\t            \"The bead body should be clear, actionable, and structured for Claude Code execution.\",\n455\t        ])\n456\t\n457\t        logger.info(f\"Formulating bead body for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n458\t\n459\t        # Make LLM call\n460\t        try:\n461\t            bead_body = await client.call_simple(\n462\t                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n463\t                user_message=user_message,\n464\t                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n465\t                max_tokens=4096,\n466\t                temperature=0.7,\n467\t            )\n468\t\n469\t            logger.info(f\"Formulated bead body for intent {request.intent_id}: {len(bead_body)} chars\")\n470\t            return bead_body\n471\t\n472\t        except Exception as e:\n473\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to formulate bead body: {e}\")\n474\t            raise EscalateError(f\"Failed to formulate bead body: {e}\") from e\n475\t\n476\t    async def create_bead(\n477\t        self,\n478\t        request: EscalateRequest,\n479\t        bead_body: str,\n480\t    ) -> str:\n481\t        \"\"\"\n482\t        Create a bead using the bf CLI.\n483\t\n484\t        Returns the bead ID.\n485\t        \"\"\"\n486\t        # Generate bead title from intent\n487\t        title = self._generate_bead_title(request)\n488\t\n489\t        # Prepare metadata (includes session_id for bead watcher routing)\n490\t        metadata = {\n491\t            \"session_id\": request.session_id,\n492\t            \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n493\t            \"intent_type\": request.intent_type,\n494\t            \"origin_surface_id\": request.metadata.get(\"surface_id\"),\n495\t            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n496\t        }\n497\t\n498\t        if request.project_slug:\n499\t            metadata[\"project_slug\"] = request.project_slug\n500\t\n501\t        if request.topic_id:\n502\t            metadata[\"topic_id\"] = request.topic_id\n503\t\n504\t        # Build bf create command\n505\t        # Using bf CLI to create the bead\n506\t        # Note: bf uses --description for the main body content\n507\t        try:\n508\t            # Build command with description (escape bead body for shell)\n509\t            cmd = [\n510\t                \"bf\",\n511\t                \"create\",\n512\t                \"--title\", title,\n513\t                \"--type\", \"task\",  # task-profile intents create task beads\n514\t                \"--description\", bead_body,\n515\t            ]\n516\t\n517\t            # Add labels for metadata tracking\n518\t            # Labels are simple strings, we'll encode key=value for structured data\n519\t            for key, value in metadata.items():\n520\t                if value is not None:\n521\t                    cmd.extend([\"--label\", f\"{key}={value}\"])\n522\t\n523\t            proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n524\t                *cmd,\n525\t                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n526\t                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n527\t            )\n528\t\n529\t            stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n530\t\n531\t            if proc.returncode != 0:\n532\t                error_msg = stderr.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n533\t                logger.error(f\"bf create failed: {error_msg}\")\n534\t                raise BeadCreationError(f\"bf create failed: {error_msg}\")\n535\t\n536\t            # Parse bead ID from output\n537\t            output = stdout.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n538\t            bead_id = self._extract_bead_id(output)\n539\t\n540\t            logger.info(f\"Created bead {bead_id} for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n541\t            return bead_id\n542\t\n543\t        except FileNotFoundError:\n544\t            logger.error(\"bf CLI not found\")\n545\t            raise BeadCreationError(\"bf CLI not found\")\n546\t        except Exception as e:\n547\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\")\n548\t            raise BeadCreationError(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\") from e\n549\t\n550\t    async def _create_bead_with_type(\n551\t        self,\n552\t        request: EscalateRequest,\n553\t        bead_body: str,\n554\t        bead_type: str,\n555\t    ) -> str:\n556\t        \"\"\"\n557\t        Create a bead with a specific type using the bf CLI.\n558\t\n559\t        Returns the bead ID.\n560\t        \"\"\"\n561\t        # Generate bead title from intent\n562\t        title = self._generate_bead_title(request)\n563\t\n564\t        # Prepare metadata (includes session_id for bead watcher routing)\n565\t        metadata = {\n566\t            \"session_id\": request.session_id,\n567\t            \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n568\t            \"intent_type\": request.intent_type,\n569\t            \"origin_surface_id\": request.metadata.get(\"surface_id\"),\n570\t            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n571\t        }\n572\t\n573\t        if request.project_slug:\n574\t            metadata[\"project_slug\"] = request.project_slug\n575\t\n576\t        if request.topic_id:\n577\t            metadata[\"topic_id\"] = request.topic_id\n578\t\n579\t        # Use utterance prefix as title\n664\t        utterance = request.utterance.strip()\n665\t        if len(utterance) > 60:\n666\t            title = utterance[:57] + \"...\"\n667\t        else:\n668\t            title = utterance\n669\t\n670\t        # Strip whitespace first\n685\t        output = output.strip()\n686\t\n687\t        if not output:\n688\t            logger.warning(f\"Empty bf create output\")\n689\t            return str(uuid.uuid4())\n690\t\n691\t        # Try to extract bead ID pattern: lowercase letters, dash, alphanumeric\n692\t        # Bead IDs typically include numbers like: abc-123, xyz-789, adc-2fs\n693\t        # We prioritize matches with digits over letter-only matches\n694\t        patterns = [\n695\t            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]*[0-9]+[a-z0-9]*\",  # Three components with digit: test-bead-456\n696\t            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]*[0-9]+[a-z0-9]*\",          # Two components with digit: abc-123\n697\t            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]+\",                  # Three components letter-only: test-bead-xyz\n698\t            r\"[a-z]{3,}-[a-z0-9]{2,}\",                   # Fallback: any 3+ char prefix, dash, 2+ char suffix\n699\t        ]\n700\t\n701\t        for pattern in patterns:\n702\t            matches = re.findall(pattern, output)\n703\t            if matches:\n704\t                # Return the longest match (prefer more specific patterns)\n705\t                bead_id = max(matches, key=len)\n706\t                logger.debug(f\"Extracted bead ID '{bead_id}' from output\")\n707\t                return bead_id\n708\t\n709\t        # Fallback: if output already looks like a bead ID, return as-is\n710\t        # Basic validation: should contain a dash and be reasonable length\n711\t        if \"-\" in output and len(output) >= 3:\n712\t            logger.warning(f\"Using output as bead ID despite non-standard format: {output}\")\n713\t            return output\n714\t\n715\t        # Last resort: return the whole output (bf might have changed format)\n716\t        logger.warning(f\"Unexpected bf create output format: {output}\")\n717\t        return output\n718\t\n719\t    def build_pending_card(\n720\t        self,\n721\t        request: EscalateRequest,\n722\t        bead_id: str,\n723\t        bead_type: str = \"task\",\n724\t    ) -> dict:\n725\t        \"\"\"\n726\t        Build a pending-card spec for the surface.\n727\t\n728\t        The pending card shows the user that work is in progress\n729\t        and will be delivered when the bead closes.\n730\t        \"\"\"\n731\t        return {\n732\t            \"type\": \"pending\",\n733\t            \"id\": f\"pending-{bead_id}\",\n734\t            \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n735\t            \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n736\t            \"title\": self._generate_bead_title(request),\n737\t            \"summary\": f\"Working on: {request.utterance[:100]}\",\n738\t            \"status\": \"pending\",\n739\t            \"urgency\": request.metadata.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n740\t            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n741\t            \"metadata\": {\n742\t                \"project_slug\": request.project_slug,\n743\t                \"topic_id\": request.topic_id,\n744\t                \"bead_type\": bead_type,\n745\t            },\n746\t        }\n747\t\n748\t    async def escalate_intent(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> EscalateResult:\n749\t        \"\"\"\n750\t        Escalate an intent to a bead.\n751\t\n752\t        Full flow:\n753\t        1. EscalateResult with bead ID and pending card (if bead created)\n763\t            or execution result (if auto-approved)\n764\t        \"\"\"\n765\t        logger.info(f\"Escalating intent {request.intent_id}\")\n766\t\n767\t        try:\n768\t            # Step 1: Load exceptions.yaml\n769\t            reload_mgr = self._get_reload_manager()\n770\t            exceptions_config = )\n787\t\n788\t                # Return result with empty pending_card (auto-approved, no bead created)\n789\t                return EscalateResult(\n790\t                    bead_id=\"\",  # No bead status=\"completed\",\n794\t                )\n795\t\n796\t            # Step 3: Determine bead type from escalation_targets\n797\t            bead_type = project_slug=request.project_slug,\n811\t                intent_type=request.intent_type,\n812\t            )\n813\t\n814\t            # Step 6: Build pending card\n815\t            pending_card = handler - handles task-profile intents requiring bead escalation.\n\nFor intents that need durable async handling:\n1. Formulate bead body via LLM (sonnet-class via ZAI proxy)\n2. Create bead using bf CLI\n3. Return pending-card spec with bead reference\n\nThe bead watcher bridges bead closure to result delivery.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport asyncio\nimport json\nimport uuid\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom datetime import datetime, timezone\nfrom enum import Enum\nfrom logging import getLogger\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any, Optional\n\nimport httpx\n\nfrom ..components.hot_reload import get_reload_manager\nfrom ..session.store import get_store\nfrom .llm import get_zai_client, LLMRequest, ModelClass\nfrom .commands import get_kubectl_executor, CommandExecutionError\n\n\nlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n\n# Project workspace for beads (where bf CLI operates)\nBEADS_WORKSPACE = Path.home() / \".beads\"\n\n# Escalate prompt path\nESCALATE_PROMPT_PATH = Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/prompts/escalate/task-profile.md\")\n\n# Default escalate system prompt (fallback if prompt file not found)\nESCALATE_SYSTEM_PROMPT = \"\"\"You are ADC's escalate handler. Your job is to formulate a clear, actionable NEEDLE bead body from a user's intent.\n\nA NEEDLE bead is a task work item with:\n- A clear title describing what needs to be done\n- A detailed body with context, requirements, and success criteria\n- Proper structure for Claude Code to execute\n\nGiven the user's intent and any available context, produce a bead body that:\n1. Captures the full scope of the request\n2. Includes relevant context (project, cluster, specific resources)\n3. Defines clear success criteria\n4. Structures the work for Claude Code execution\n\nOutput ONLY the bead body as markdown. Do not include explanations or meta-commentary.\"\"\"\n\n\ndef load_escalate_prompt() -> str:\n    \"\"\"Load the escalate prompt from the prompts directory.\"\"\"\n    try:\n        with open(ESCALATE_PROMPT_PATH, \"r\") as f:\n            content = f.read()\n            if content.strip():\n                return content\n            else:\n                logger.warning(f\"Escalate prompt file is empty: {ESCALATE_PROMPT_PATH}, using default\")\n                return ESCALATE_SYSTEM_PROMPT\n    except FileNotFoundError:\n        logger.warning(f\"Escalate prompt not found at {ESCALATE_PROMPT_PATH}, using default\")\n        return ESCALATE_SYSTEM_PROMPT\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass EscalateRequest:\n    \"\"\"Request to escalate an intent to a bead.\"\"\"\n    intent_id: str\n    session_id: str\n    utterance: str\n    intent_type: str\n    project_slug: str | None = None\n    topic_id: str | None = None\n    context: dict = field(default_factory=dict)\n    metadata: dict = field(default_factory=dict)\n\n    def to_dict(self) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Convert to dictionary.\"\"\"\n        return {\n            \"intent_id\": self.intent_id,\n            \"session_id\": self.session_id,\n            \"utterance\": self.utterance,\n            \"intent_type\": self.intent_type,\n            \"project_slug\": self.project_slug,\n            \"topic_id\": self.topic_id,\n            \"context\": self.context,\n            \"metadata\": self.metadata,\n        }\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass EscalateResult:\n    \"\"\"Result of escalating an intent to a bead.\"\"\"\n    bead_id: str\n    intent_id: str\n    pending_card: dict\n    status: str = \"pending\"  # pending, created, failed\n\n    def to_dict(self) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Convert to dictionary.\"\"\"\n        return {\n            \"bead_id\": self.bead_id,\n            \"intent_id\": self.intent_id,\n            \"pending_card\": self.pending_card,\n            \"status\": self.status,\n        }\n\n\nclass EscalateError(Exception):\n    \"\"\"Base exception for escalate errors.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n\nclass BeadCreationError(EscalateError):\n    \"\"\"Bead creation failed.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n\nclass EscalateHandler:\n    \"\"\"\n    Escalate handler for task-profile intents.\n\n    Handles the full escalate flow:\n    1. Check auto-approve rules from exceptions.yaml\n    2. Either execute directly (auto-approved) OR create bead (manual approval)\n    3. Return pending-card spec (if bead created) or execution result\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, store=None):\n        self.store = store\n        self._zai_client = None\n        self._reload_manager = None\n\n    async def _get_zai_client(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create ZAI client.\"\"\"\n        if self._zai_client is None:\n            self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n        return self._zai_client\n\n    async def _get_store(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create session store.\"\"\"\n        if self.store is None:\n            self.store = get_store()\n        return self.store\n\n    def _get_reload_manager(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create hot-reload manager.\"\"\"\n        if self._reload_manager is None:\n            self._reload_manager = get_reload_manager()\n        return self._reload_manager\n\n    def _evaluate_auto_approve(self, request: EscalateRequest, exceptions_config: dict) -> tuple[bool, str | None]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Evaluate if an intent should be auto-approved based on exceptions.yaml.\n\n        Args:\n            request: The escalate request\n            exceptions_config: The loaded exceptions.yaml config\n\n        Returns:\n            (auto_approve: bool, reason: str | None)\n        \"\"\"\n        auto_approve_rules = exceptions_config.get(\"auto_approve\", {})\n\n        # Extract context for rule evaluation\n        context = {\n            \"environment\": request.metadata.get(\"environment\"),\n            \"project_slug\": request.project_slug,\n            \"branch\": request.metadata.get(\"branch\"),\n            \"action\": request.metadata.get(\"action\"),\n            \"risk_level\": request.metadata.get(\"risk_level\", \"normal\"),\n        }\n\n        # Check read-only operations (always auto-approved)\n        read_only_actions = auto_approve_rules.get(\"read_only\", [])\n        action = context.get(\"action\")\n        if action in read_only_actions:\n            return True, f\"Read-only operation: {action}\"\n\n        # Check safe mutations with conditions\n        safe_mutations = auto_approve_rules.get(\"safe_mutations\", [])\n        for rule in safe_mutations:\n            condition = rule.get(\"condition\", \"\")\n            actions = rule.get(\"actions\", [])\n\n            # Simple condition evaluation (supports basic comparisons)\n            if self._evaluate_condition(condition, context) and action in actions:\n                return True, f\"Safe mutation in {condition}: {action}\"\n\n        # Check manual approval rules (never auto-approve)\n        manual_rules = exceptions_config.get(\"manual_approval\", [])\n        for rule in manual_rules:\n            condition = rule.get(\"condition\", \"\")\n            actions = rule.get(\"actions\", [])\n\n            # Check if the action is in this rule's actions\n            if action not in actions:\n                continue\n\n            # Evaluate the condition\n            if self._evaluate_condition(condition, context):\n                if rule.get(\"always_approve\") is False:\n                    return False, f\"Manual approval required: {condition}\"\n\n        # Check approval workflow rules\n        approval_config = exceptions_config.get(\"approval\", {})\n        never_auto_approve = approval_config.get(\"never_auto_approve\", [])\n        for condition_str in never_auto_approve:\n            if self._evaluate_condition(condition_str, context):\n                return False, f\"Never auto-approve: {condition_str}\"\n\n        # Default: require manual approval for unknown actions\n        return False, \"Unknown action - requires manual approval\"\n\n    def _evaluate_condition(self, condition: str, context: dict) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Evaluate a condition string against context.\n\n        Supports simple conditions like:\n        - \"environment == 'staging'\"\n        - \"branch == 'main' || branch == 'master'\"  (using || for OR)\n        - \"action == 'kubectl_delete_namespace'\"\n        - \"environment == 'staging' && action == 'restart'\"  (using && for AND)\n\n        Note: Supports both shell-style operators (||, &&) and Python-style (or, and).\n\n        Args:\n            condition: The condition string\n            context: Dict of available variables\n\n        Returns:\n            True if condition evaluates to True, False otherwise\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            # Translate shell-style operators to Python syntax\n            # (People are more familiar with || and && from bash/js)\n            python_condition = condition.replace(\" || \", \" or \").replace(\" && \", \" and \")\n\n            # Create a safe evaluation environment\n            safe_globals = {\"__builtins__\": {}}\n            safe_locals = {\n                \"environment\": context.get(\"environment\"),\n                \"project_slug\": context.get(\"project_slug\"),\n                \"branch\": context.get(\"branch\"),\n                \"action\": context.get(\"action\"),\n                \"risk_level\": context.get(\"risk_level\"),\n            }\n\n            # Evaluate condition\n            result = eval(python_condition, safe_globals, safe_locals)\n            return bool(result)\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to evaluate condition '{condition}': {e}\")\n            return False\n\n    def _get_bead_type_from_targets(self, intent_type: str, exceptions_config: dict) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Get the bead type from escalation_targets config.\n\n        Args:\n            intent_type: The intent type from IntentRouter (e.g., 'action', 'task-profile', 'self-modification')\n            exceptions_config: The loaded exceptions.yaml config\n\n        Returns:\n            The bead type to create (default: 'task')\n        \"\"\"\n        # Map IntentRouter types to escalation_targets keys\n        intent_type_mapping = {\n            \"action\": \"action\",\n            \"task-profile\": \"action\",  # Task-profile intents create action-type beads\n            \"self-modification\": \"self_modification\",\n            \"monitoring-config\": \"monitoring_config\",\n        }\n\n        # Map the intent type to escalation_targets key\n        escalation_key = intent_type_mapping.get(intent_type, intent_type)\n\n        escalation_targets = exceptions_config.get(\"escalation_targets\", {})\n        target_config = escalation_targets.get(escalation_key, {})\n        return target_config.get(\"bead_type\", \"task\")\n\n    async def _execute_auto_approved(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Execute an auto-approved action directly.\n\n        Executes kubectl and git commands that are auto-approved based on\n        exceptions.yaml rules, without creating beads.\n\n        Args:\n            request: The escalate request\n\n        Returns:\n            Execution result dict\n        \"\"\"\n        action = request.metadata.get(\"action\", \"\")\n        logger.info(f\"Executing auto-approved action '{action}' for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n\n        try:\n            # Route to appropriate executor based on action type\n            if action == \"kubectl_delete_pod\":\n                return await self._execute_delete_pod(request)\n            elif action.startswith(\"kubectl_\"):\n                return await self._execute_kubectl_command(request)\n            elif action.startswith(\"git_\"):\n                return await self._execute_git_command(request)\n            else:\n                # Unknown action - return placeholder for now\n                logger.warning(f\"Unknown auto-approved action: {action}\")\n                return {\n                    \"status\": \"pending\",\n                    \"summary\": f\"Auto-approved action '{action}' not yet implemented\",\n                    \"data\": {\n                        \"action\": action,\n                        \"utterance\": request.utterance,\n                    },\n                    \"urgency\": \"low\",\n                }\n\n        except CommandExecutionError as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Command execution failed for intent {request.intent_id}: {e}\")\n            return {\n                \"status\": \"failed\",\n                \"summary\": f\"Command execution failed: {str(e)}\",\n                \"data\": {\n                    \"action\": action,\n                    \"error\": str(e),\n                },\n                \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n            }\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Unexpected error executing auto-approved action: {e}\")\n            return {\n                \"status\": \"failed\",\n                \"summary\": f\"Unexpected error: {str(e)}\",\n                \"data\": {\n                    \"action\": action,\n                    \"error\": str(e),\n                },\n                \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n            }\n\n    async def _execute_delete_pod(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Execute kubectl delete pod command.\n\n        Args:\n            request: The escalate request\n\n        Returns:\n            Execution result dict\n        \"\"\"\n        executor = get_kubectl_executor()\n\n        # Parse utterance to extract pod name and namespace\n        params = executor.parse_delete_pod_utterance(\n            utterance=request.utterance,\n            project_slug=request.project_slug,\n        )\n\n        # Execute the delete command\n        result = await executor.execute_delete_pod(\n            pod_name=params[\"pod_name\"],\n            namespace=params[\"namespace\"],\n            project_slug=request.project_slug,\n        )\n\n        return result\n\n    async def _execute_kubectl_command(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Execute other kubectl commands (placeholder for future).\n\n        Args:\n            request: The escalate request\n\n        Returns:\n            Execution result dict\n        \"\"\"\n        # TODO: Implement other kubectl commands\n        action = request.metadata.get(\"action\", \"\")\n        return {\n            \"status\": \"pending\",\n            \"summary\": f\"Kubectl command '{action}' not yet implemented\",\n            \"data\": {\n                \"action\": action,\n                \"utterance\": request.utterance,\n            },\n            \"urgency\": \"low\",\n        }\n\n    async def _execute_git_command(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Execute git commands (placeholder for future).\n\n        Args:\n            request: The escalate request\n\n        Returns:\n            Execution result dict\n        \"\"\"\n        # TODO: Implement git commands\n        action = request.metadata.get(\"action\", \"\")\n        return {\n            \"status\": \"pending\",\n            \"summary\": f\"Git command '{action}' not yet implemented\",\n            \"data\": {\n                \"action\": action,\n                \"utterance\": request.utterance,\n            },\n            \"urgency\": \"low\",\n        }\n\n    async def formulate_bead_body(\n        self,\n        request: EscalateRequest,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Formulate a bead body using the LLM.\n\n        Takes the intent and context, produces a well-structured bead body.\n        \"\"\"\n        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n        system_prompt = load_escalate_prompt()\n\n        # Build context for the LLM\n        context_parts = [\n            f\"User utterance: {request.utterance}\",\n            f\"Intent type: {request.intent_type}\",\n        ]\n\n        if request.project_slug:\n            context_parts.append(f\"Project: {request.project_slug}\")\n\n        if request.topic_id:\n            context_parts.append(f\"Topic ID: {request.topic_id}\")\n\n        # Add any additional context\n        if request.context:\n            context_parts.append(f\"\\nAdditional context:\")\n            for key, value in request.context.items():\n                context_parts.append(f\"  {key}: {value}\")\n\n        # Build the user message\n        user_message = \"\\n\".join([\n            \"Formulate a NEEDLE bead body for this intent:\",\n            \"\",\n            *context_parts,\n            \"\",\n            \"The bead body should be clear, actionable, and structured for Claude Code execution.\",\n        ])\n\n        logger.info(f\"Formulating bead body for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n\n        # Make LLM call\n        try:\n            bead_body = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n                max_tokens=4096,\n                temperature=0.7,\n            )\n\n            logger.info(f\"Formulated bead body for intent {request.intent_id}: {len(bead_body)} chars\")\n            return bead_body\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to formulate bead body: {e}\")\n            raise EscalateError(f\"Failed to formulate bead body: {e}\") from e\n\n    async def create_bead(\n        self,\n        request: EscalateRequest,\n        bead_body: str,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Create a bead using the bf CLI.\n\n        Returns the bead ID.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Generate bead title from intent\n        title = self._generate_bead_title(request)\n\n        # Prepare metadata (includes session_id for bead watcher routing)\n        metadata = {\n            \"session_id\": request.session_id,\n            \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n            \"intent_type\": request.intent_type,\n            \"origin_surface_id\": request.metadata.get(\"surface_id\"),\n            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n        }\n\n        if request.project_slug:\n            metadata[\"project_slug\"] = request.project_slug\n\n        if request.topic_id:\n            metadata[\"topic_id\"] = request.topic_id\n\n        # Build bf create command\n        # Using bf CLI to create the bead\n        # Note: bf uses --description for the main body content\n        try:\n            # Build command with description (escape bead body for shell)\n            cmd = [\n                \"bf\",\n                \"create\",\n                \"--title\", title,\n                \"--type\", \"task\",  # task-profile intents create task beads\n                \"--description\", bead_body,\n            ]\n\n            # Add labels for metadata tracking\n            # Labels are simple strings, we'll encode key=value for structured data\n            for key, value in metadata.items():\n                if value is not None:\n                    cmd.extend([\"--label\", f\"{key}={value}\"])\n\n            proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                *cmd,\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n\n            stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n\n            if proc.returncode != 0:\n                error_msg = stderr.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n                logger.error(f\"bf create failed: {error_msg}\")\n                raise BeadCreationError(f\"bf create failed: {error_msg}\")\n\n            # Parse bead ID from output\n            output = stdout.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n            bead_id = self._extract_bead_id(output)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Created bead {bead_id} for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n            return bead_id\n\n        except FileNotFoundError:\n            logger.error(\"bf CLI not found\")\n            raise BeadCreationError(\"bf CLI not found\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\")\n            raise BeadCreationError(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\") from e\n\n    async def _create_bead_with_type(\n        self,\n        request: EscalateRequest,\n        bead_body: str,\n        bead_type: str,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Create a bead with a specific type using the bf CLI.\n\n        Returns the bead ID.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Generate bead title from intent\n        title = self._generate_bead_title(request)\n\n        # Prepare metadata (includes session_id for bead watcher routing)\n        metadata = {\n            \"session_id\": request.session_id,\n            \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n            \"intent_type\": request.intent_type,\n            \"origin_surface_id\": request.metadata.get(\"surface_id\"),\n            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n        }\n\n        if request.project_slug:\n            metadata[\"project_slug\"] = request.project_slug\n\n        if request.topic_id:\n            metadata[\"topic_id\"] = request.topic_id\n\n        # Build bf create command\n        try:\n            cmd = [\n                \"bf\",\n                \"create\",\n                \"--title\", title,\n                \"--type\", bead_type,\n                \"--description\", bead_body,\n            ]\n\n            # Add labels for metadata tracking\n            for key, value in metadata.items():\n                if value is not None:\n                    cmd.extend([\"--label\", f\"{key}={value}\"])\n\n            proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                *cmd,\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n\n            stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n\n            if proc.returncode != 0:\n                error_msg = stderr.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n                logger.error(f\"bf create failed: {error_msg}\")\n                raise BeadCreationError(f\"bf create failed: {error_msg}\")\n\n            # Parse bead ID from output\n            output = stdout.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n            bead_id = self._extract_bead_id(output)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Created bead {bead_id} (type: {bead_type}) for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n            return bead_id\n\n        except FileNotFoundError:\n            logger.error(\"bf CLI not found\")\n            raise BeadCreationError(\"bf CLI not found\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\")\n            raise BeadCreationError(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\") from e\n\n    async def _create_bead_watch(\n        self,\n        bead_ref: str,\n        project_slug: str | None,\n        intent_type: str,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Create bead_watch row for circuit breaker tracking.\n\n        Plan §10 The Async Path: watcher tracks open beads for refusals/SLA.\n        SLA defaults by intent_type, with per-project sla_overrides per intent_type.\n\n        Args:\n            bead_ref: The bead ID to watch\n            project_slug: Project slug for SLA override lookup\n            intent_type: Intent type for default SLA lookup\n        \"\"\"\n        from ..registry import get_project\n\n        sla_hours = None\n\n        # Check for per-project, per-intent-type SLA override\n        if project_slug:\n            project = get_project(project_slug)\n            if project:\n                sla_overrides = project.get(\"sla_overrides\", {})\n                if sla_overrides and intent_type in sla_overrides:\n                    sla_hours = sla_overrides.get(intent_type)\n                    logger.info(\n                        f\"Using per-project SLA override for {project_slug}/{intent_type}: \"\n                        f\"{sla_hours} hours\"\n                    )\n\n        store = await self._get_store()\n        await store.create_bead_watch(\n            bead_ref=bead_ref,\n            sla_hours=sla_hours,\n            intent_type=intent_type,\n        )\n        logger.debug(f\"Created bead_watch row for {bead_ref}\")\n\n    def _generate_bead_title(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Generate a bead title from the request.\"\"\"\n        # Use utterance prefix as title\n        utterance = request.utterance.strip()\n        if len(utterance) > 60:\n            title = utterance[:57] + \"...\"\n        else:\n            title = utterance\n\n        # Add project prefix if available\n        if request.project_slug:\n            title = f\"[{request.project_slug}] {title}\"\n\n        return title\n\n    def _extract_bead_id(self, output: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Extract bead ID from bf create output.\n\n        bf create outputs just the bead ID (e.g., \"abc-123\").\n        May include prefix text like \"Created bead abc-123\" in some versions.\n        \"\"\"\n        import re\n\n        # Strip whitespace first\n        output = output.strip()\n\n        if not output:\n            logger.warning(f\"Empty bf create output\")\n            return str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n        # Try to extract bead ID pattern: lowercase letters, dash, alphanumeric\n        # Bead IDs typically include numbers like: abc-123, xyz-789, adc-2fs\n        # We prioritize matches with digits over letter-only matches\n        patterns = [\n            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]*[0-9]+[a-z0-9]*\",  # Three components with digit: test-bead-456\n            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]*[0-9]+[a-z0-9]*\",          # Two components with digit: abc-123\n            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]+\",                  # Three components letter-only: test-bead-xyz\n            r\"[a-z]{3,}-[a-z0-9]{2,}\",                   # Fallback: any 3+ char prefix, dash, 2+ char suffix\n        ]\n\n        for pattern in patterns:\n            matches = re.findall(pattern, output)\n            if matches:\n                # Return the longest match (prefer more specific patterns)\n                bead_id = max(matches, key=len)\n                logger.debug(f\"Extracted bead ID '{bead_id}' from output\")\n                return bead_id\n\n        # Fallback: if output already looks like a bead ID, return as-is\n        # Basic validation: should contain a dash and be reasonable length\n        if \"-\" in output and len(output) >= 3:\n            logger.warning(f\"Using output as bead ID despite non-standard format: {output}\")\n            return output\n\n        # Last resort: return the whole output (bf might have changed format)\n        logger.warning(f\"Unexpected bf create output format: {output}\")\n        return output\n\n    def build_pending_card(\n        self,\n        request: EscalateRequest,\n        bead_id: str,\n        bead_type: str = \"task\",\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Build a pending-card spec for the surface.\n\n        The pending card shows the user that work is in progress\n        and will be delivered when the bead closes.\n        \"\"\"\n        return {\n            \"type\": \"pending\",\n            \"id\": f\"pending-{bead_id}\",\n            \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n            \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n            \"title\": self._generate_bead_title(request),\n            \"summary\": f\"Working on: {request.utterance[:100]}\",\n            \"status\": \"pending\",\n            \"urgency\": request.metadata.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n            \"metadata\": {\n                \"project_slug\": request.project_slug,\n                \"topic_id\": request.topic_id,\n                \"bead_type\": bead_type,\n            },\n        }\n\n    async def escalate_intent(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> EscalateResult:\n        \"\"\"\n        Escalate an intent to a bead.\n\n        Full flow:\n        1. Load exceptions.yaml to determine bead type from escalation_targets\n        2. Evaluate auto-approve rules from exceptions.yaml\n        3. If auto-approved: execute directly and return result\n        4. Otherwise: formulate bead body via LLM, create bead, return pending-card spec\n\n        Args:\n            request: The escalate request\n\n        Returns:\n            EscalateResult with bead ID and pending card (if bead created)\n            or execution result (if auto-approved)\n        \"\"\"\n        logger.info(f\"Escalating intent {request.intent_id}\")\n\n        try:\n            # Step 1: Load exceptions.yaml\n            reload_mgr = self._get_reload_manager()\n            exceptions_config = reload_mgr.get_config(\"exceptions\")\n\n            # Step 2: Evaluate auto-approve rules\n            auto_approve, reason = self._evaluate_auto_approve(request, exceptions_config)\n\n            if auto_approve:\n                logger.info(f\"Auto-approved intent {request.intent_id}: {reason}\")\n\n                # Execute directly without bead creation\n                execution_result = await self._execute_auto_approved(request)\n\n                # Update intent in store\n                store = await self._get_store()\n                await store.update_intent_status(\n                    intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                    status=\"resolved\",\n                )\n\n                # Return result with empty pending_card (auto-approved, no bead created)\n                return EscalateResult(\n                    bead_id=\"\",  # No bead created\n                    intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                    pending_card=execution_result,  # Return execution result directly\n                    status=\"completed\",\n                )\n\n            # Step 3: Determine bead type from escalation_targets\n            bead_type = self._get_bead_type_from_targets(request.intent_type, exceptions_config)\n            logger.info(f\"Creating bead type '{bead_type}' for intent {request.intent_id} (not auto-approved: {reason})\")\n\n            # Step 4: Formulate bead body\n            bead_body = await self.formulate_bead_body(request)\n\n            # Step 5: Create bead with determined type\n            bead_id = await self._create_bead_with_type(request, bead_body, bead_type)\n\n            # Step 5.5: Create bead_watch row for circuit breaker tracking\n            # (plan §10 The Async Path: watcher tracks open beads for refusals/SLA)\n            await self._create_bead_watch(\n                bead_ref=bead_id,\n                project_slug=request.project_slug,\n                intent_type=request.intent_type,\n            )\n\n            # Step 6: Build pending card\n            pending_card = self.build_pending_card(request, bead_id, bead_type)\n\n            # Update intent in store with bead reference\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.update_intent_status(\n                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                status=\"dispatched\",\n            )\n\n            logger.info(f\"Escalated intent {request.intent_id} to bead {bead_id}\")\n\n            return EscalateResult(\n                bead_id=bead_id,\n                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                pending_card=pending_card,\n                status=\"created\",\n            )\n\n        except EscalateError:\n            raise\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Escalate failed for intent {request.intent_id}: {e}\")\n            raise EscalateError(f\"Escalate failed: {e}\") from e\n\n\n# Global escalate handler instance\n_handler: Optional[EscalateHandler] = None\n\n\ndef get_escalate_handler(store=None) -> EscalateHandler:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the global escalate handler instance.\"\"\"\n    global _handler\n    if _handler is None:\n        _handler = EscalateHandler(store=store)\n    return _handler\n\n\nasync def escalate_intent(request: EscalateRequest) -> EscalateResult:\n    \"\"\"\n    Convenience function to escalate an intent.\n\n    Args:\n        request: The escalate request\n\n    Returns:\n        EscalateResult with bead ID and pending card\n    \"\"\"\n    handler = get_escalate_handler()\n    return await handler.escalate_intent(request)\n","numLines":864,"startLine":1,"totalLines":864}}}

     assert newer > older\n178\t\n179\t\n180\t# --- bf subprocess wrapper: _run_bf_list_closed (adc-qw85) ------------------\n181\t\n182\t\n183\tclass _FakeProc:\n184\t    \"\"\"Stand-in for the asyncio subprocess returned by create_subprocess_exec.\"\"\"\n185\t\n186\t    def __init__(self, stdout=b\"\", stderr=b\"\", returncode=0, communicate_fn=None):\n187\t        self._stdout = communicate(self):\n194\t        if self._communicate_fn is not None:\n195\t            return await self._communicate_fn()\n196\t        return self._stdout, self._stderr\n197\t\n198\t    def kill(self):\n199\t        self.killed = True\n200\t\n201\t    async def wait(self):\n202\t        return self.returncode\n203\t\n204\t\n205\tdef _patch_subprocess(monkeypatch, proc=None, *, Asserts the watcher invokes bf from a real workspace cwd.\"\"\"\n208\t    async def fake_exec(*args, **kwargs):\n209\t        if raises is not None:\n210\t            raise raises\n211\t        assert kwargs.get(\"cwd\") is not None, \"bf must run from its workspace cwd\"\n212\t        return proc\n213\t\n214\t    monkeypatch.setattr(\"src.watcher.daemon.asyncio.create_subprocess_exec\", No real subprocess: keep the tick hermetic and empty.\n727\t        async def no_records():\n728\t            return []\n729\t\n730\t        w._run_bf_list_closed = no_records\n731\t\n732\t        # Patch asyncio.sleep in the daemon module: the loop's interval sleep\n733\t        # (seconds < 1.0, since we set the interval to 0.0) becomes instant and\n734\t        # terminates the task after 3 completed ticks, so the test is\n735\t        # deterministic and never waits real time.\n736\t        real_sleep = asyncio.sleep\n737\t        interval_sleeps = {\"n\": 0}\n738\t\n739\t        async def fast_sleep(seconds):\n740\t            await real_sleep(0)  # yield to the scheduler\n741\t            if seconds < 1.0:\n742\t                interval_sleeps[\"n\"] += 1\n743\t                if interval_sleeps[\"n\"] >= 3:\n744\t                    raise asyncio.CancelledError()  # The supervisor\n765\t    layer now respawns it on death with exponential backoff (2s, 4s, 8s, capped).\n766\t    \"\"\"\n767\t\n768\t    async def test_crashed_task_restarts_with_increasing_backoff(\n769\t        self, store, router, monkeypatch, caplog\n770\t    ):\n771\t        w = BeadWatcher(store, router, check_interval_seconds=0.0)\n772\t\n773\t        # Every tick crashes the task outright (escapes the transient catch).\n774\t        async def crash():\n775\t            raise _TaskCrash(\"forced crash\")\n776\t\n777\t        monkeypatch.setattr(w, \"_check_for_events\", crash)\n778\t\n779\t        # Capture the backoff durations the supervisor requests, without really\n780\t        # sleeping. After the 3rd backoff sleep, stop the supervisor so the test\n781\t        # terminates deterministically.\n782\t        real_sleep = asyncio.sleep\n783\t        requested = []\n784\t\n785\t        async def capture_sleep(seconds):\n786\t            requested.append(seconds)\n787\t            await real_sleep(0)\n788\t            if seconds >= 1.0 and sum(1 for s in requested if s >= 1.0) >= 3:\n789\t                raise asyncio.CancelledError()\n790\t\n791\t        monkeypatch.setattr(\"src.watcher.daemon.asyncio.sleep\", capture_sleep)\n792\t\n793\t        with caplog.at_level(logging.WARNING, logger=\"src.watcher.daemon\"):\n794\t            await w.start()\n795\t            for _ in range(200):\n796\t                if w._supervisor_task is None or w._supervisor_task.done():\n797\t                    break\n798\t                await real_sleep(0.002)\n799\t            await w.stop()\n800\t\n801\t        backoffs = [s for s in requested if s >= 1.0]\n802\t        # Three crashes, each followed by a logged restart with growing backoff.\n803\t        assert len(backoffs) >= 3\n804\t        assert backoffs[0] == pytest.approx(2.0)\n805\t        assert backoffs[1] == pytest.approx(4.0)\n806\t        assert backoffs[2] == pytest.approx(8.0)\n807\t        assert w._restart_count >= 3\n808\t        restart_logs = [r for r in caplog.records if \"restarting\" in r.getMessage().lower()]\n809\t        assert len(restart_logs) >= 3\n810\t\n811\t    async def test_transient_exception_does_not_trigger_restart(\n812\t        self, store, router, monkeypatch\n813\t    ):\n814\t        \"\"\"A normal Exception is caught by the loop -- no supervisor restart.\n815\t\n816\t        Pins the two-layer split: transient per-iteration errors stay absorbed\n817\t        by the watch loop (pre-supervisor behavior preserved); only a real task\n818\t        death reaches the supervisor. So no backoff is ever requested and the\n819\t        task keeps ticking despite the recurring error.\n820\t        \"\"\"\n821\t        w = BeadWatcher(store, router, check_interval_seconds=0.0)\n822\t\n823\t        async def transient():\n824\t            raise RuntimeError(\"transient per-iteration error\")\n825\t\n826\t        monkeypatch.setattr(w, \"_check_for_events\", transient)\n827\t\n828\t        real_sleep = asyncio.sleep\n829\t        sleeps = []\n830\t\n831\t        async def fast_sleep(seconds):\n832\t            sleeps.append(seconds)\n833\t            await real_sleep(0)\n834\t            if seconds < 1.0 and len([s for s in sleeps if s < 1.0]) >= 4:\n835\t                raise asyncio.CancelledError()\n836\t\n837\t        monkeypatch.setattr(\"src.watcher.daemon.asyncio.sleep\", fast_sleep)\n838\t\n839\t        await w.start()\n840\t        for _ in range(200):\n841\t            if w._supervisor_task is None or w._supervisor_task.done():\n842\t                break\n843\t            await real_sleep(0.002)\n844\t        await w.stop()\n845\t\n846\t        # No backoff (>= 1.0) sleep was ever requested, and the task never died.\n847\t        assert not any(s >= 1.0 for s in sleeps)\n848\t        assert w._restart_count == 0\n849\t        # The loop kept ticking -- liveness advanced despite the errors.\n850\t        assert w.tick_count >= 4\n851\t\n852\t\n853\t# --- real bf end-to-end (skip if bf absent) (adc-qw85) ----------------------\n854\t\n855\t\n856\t@pytest.fixture\n857\tdef bf_workspace(tmp_path):\n858\t    \"\"\"An isolated, initialized bead-forge workspace for e2e detection.\"\"\"\n859\t    if not shutil.which(\"bf\"):\n860\t        pytest.skip(\"bf (bead-forge) CLI not on PATH\")\n861\t    ws = tmp_path / \"ws\"\n862\t    ws.mkdir()\n863\t    try:\n864\t        subprocess.run(\n865\t            [\"bf\", \"init\", \"-w\", str(ws)], check=True, capture_output=True, timeout=20\n866\t        )\n867\t    except (subprocess.CalledProcessError, subprocess.TimeoutExpired) as e:\n868\t        pytest.skip(f\"could not bf init a scratch workspace: {e!r}\")\n869\t    return ws\n870\t\n871\t\n872\tdef _bf_run(ws, *args):\n873\t    \"\"\"Run a bf subcommand in workspace ``ws`` and return its stdout.\"\"\"\n874\t    return subprocess.run(\n875\t        [\"bf\", *args, \"-w\", str(ws)],\n876\t        check=True,\n877\t        capture_output=True,\n878\t        text=True,\n879\t        timeout=20,\n880\t    ).stdout\n881\t\n882\t\n883\tclass TestRealBfEndToEnd:\n884\t    \"\"\"Exercises the real CLI + parser. stderr=b\"\", returncode=0, communicate_fn=None):\n        self._stdout = stdout\n        self._stderr = stderr\n        self.returncode = returncode\n        self._communicate_fn = communicate_fn\n        self.killed = False\n\n    async def communicate(self):\n        if self._communicate_fn is not None:\n            return await self._communicate_fn()\n        return self._stdout, self._stderr\n\n    def kill(self):\n        self.killed = True\n\n    async def wait(self):\n        return self.returncode\n\n\ndef _patch_subprocess(monkeypatch, proc=None, *, raises=None):\n    \"\"\"Patch create_subprocess_exec in the daemon module to return ``proc`` (or\n    raise ``raises``). Asserts the watcher invokes bf from a real workspace cwd.\"\"\"\n    async def fake_exec(*args, **kwargs):\n        if raises is not None:\n            raise raises\n        assert kwargs.get(\"cwd\") is not None, \"bf must run from its workspace cwd\"\n        return proc\n\n    monkeypatch.setattr(\"src.watcher.daemon.asyncio.create_subprocess_exec\", fake_exec)\n\n\nclass TestRunBfListClosed:\n    \"\"\"The CLI subprocess wrapper: happy path + every failure mode is non-fatal.\"\"\"\n\n    async def test_parses_jsonl_records(self, store, router, monkeypatch):\n        out = (\n returncode=0))\n        w = BeadWatcher(store, router)\n        recs = await w._run_bf_list_closed()\n        assert [r[\"id\"] for r in recs] == [\"adc-a\", \"adc-b\"]\n\n    async def ---------------\n\n\ndef _watcher_with_records(store, router, records, *, highwater=None):\n    \"\"\"A BeadWatcher whose bf subprocess is stubbed to return ``records``.\"\"\"\n    w = BeadWatcher(store, router)\n\n    async def fake_run():\n        return list(records)\n\n    w._run_bf_list_closed = fake_run  # instance override; no real subprocess\n    if highwater is not None:\n        w._close_highwater = highwater\n    return w\n\n\nclass TestHighWaterMark:\n    \"\"\"close-timestamp HWM: baseline on the first tick, emit only strictly-newer.\"\"\"\n\n    async def test_first_tick_baselines_and_emits_nothing(self, store, router):\n        recs = [_bead(bead_id=\"adc-old\", closed_at=\"2026-07-19T22:42:37.385045363Z\")]\n        w = _watcher_with_records(store, router, recs)\n        assert w._close_highwater is None\n        ev = await w._poll_closed_beads()\n        assert ev == []\n        # Baselined to the backlog's newest close time.\n        assert w._close_highwater == pytest.approx(\n            BeadWatcher._parse_close_epoch(\"2026-07-19T22:42:37.385045363Z\")\n        )\n\n    async def test_emits_only_strictly_newer_closure(self, store, BeadWatcher._parse_close_epoch(\"2026-07-19T22:42:37Z\")\n        w = _watcher_with_records(store, router, [old, new], highwater=hw)\n        ev = await w._poll_closed_beads()\n        assert TestSendToTelegram:\n    \"\"\"_send_to_telegram routes through TelegramFallback instead of being a no-op.\n\n    adc-372c: the bead watcher's bead-close -> Telegram path used to log a\n    warning and return without ever calling the bridge. It now delivers via the\n    shared TelegramFallback.send_message using the configured chat_id, with the\n    same graceful no-op-when-unconfigured behavior as send_exception().\n    \"\"\"\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def _fallback_with_chat_id(chat_id, send_return=True):\n        \"\"\"A TelegramFallback whose send_message is replaced with a capturing fake.\"\"\"\n        fb = TelegramFallback(chat_id=chat_id, bridge_url=\"http://test-bridge:8000\")\n        sent = []\n\n        async def fake_send_message(chat_id, message, parse_mode=\"HTML\"):\n            sent.append((chat_id, message, parse_mode))\n            return send_return\n\n        fb.send_message = fake_send_message\n        return fb, sent\n\n    async def test_delivers_to_configured_chat_id(self, store, router):\n        fb, sent = self._fallback_with_chat_id(chat_id=4242)\n        w = BeadWatcher(store, router, telegram_fallback=fb)\n\n        result = {\n            \"summary\": \"Bead adc-1 done\",\n            \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n            \"data\": {\"bead_id\": \"adc-1\"},\n        }\n        ok = await w._send_to_telegram(result, \"session-1\")\n\n        assert ok is True\n        assert len(sent) == 1\n        assert sent[0][0] == 4242  # routed to the configured chat id\n        # Body is produced by _format_telegram_message.\n        assert \"Bead adc-1 done\" in sent[0][1]\n\n    async def test_returns_false_on_bridge_failure(self, store, router):\n        \"\"\"A real call is made; the bridge's failure propagates, not a hard-coded value.\"\"\"\n        fb, sent = self._fallback_with_chat_id(chat_id=1, send_return=False)\n        w = BeadWatcher(store, router, telegram_fallback=fb)\n\n        ok = await w._send_to_telegram({\"summary\": \"hi\"}, \"session-1\")\n\n        assert ok is False\n        assert len(sent) == 1  # the call was actually attempted\n\n    async def test_no_op_without_chat_id(self, store, router, caplog):\n        \"\"\"No chat id configured -> graceful WARNING + False, no send attempted.\"\"\"\n        fb = TelegramFallback(bridge_url=\"http://test-bridge:8000\")\n        assert fb.chat_id is None\n        # Sentinel: if delivery were attempted, this would raise.\n\n        async def boom(*a, **kw):\n            raise AssertionError(\"send_message must not be called when chat_id is None\")\n\n        fb.send_message = boom\n        w = BeadWatcher(store, router, telegram_fallback=fb)\n\n        with caplog.at_level(\"WARNING\"):\n            ok = await w._send_to_telegram({\"summary\": \"hi\"}, \"session-1\")\n\n        assert ok is False\n        warnings = [r for r in caplog.records if r.levelname == \"WARNING\"]\n        assert len(warnings) == 1\n        assert \"ADC_TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID\" in warnings[0].message\n\n    async def test_uses_injected_fallback_not_singleton(self, store, router, monkeypatch):\n        \"\"\"The injected fallback wins over the module singleton.\"\"\"\n        # Poison the singleton so any accidental use of it is caught.\n\n        async def boom(*a, **kw):\n            raise AssertionError(\"injected fallback must be used, not the singleton\")\n\n        import src.watcher.daemon as daemon_mod\n\n        singleton = TelegramFallback(chat_id=1)\n        singleton.send_message = boom\n        monkeypatch.setattr(daemon_mod, \"get_telegram_fallback\", lambda: singleton)\n\n        fb, sent = self._fallback_with_chat_id(chat_id=4242)\n        w = BeadWatcher(store, router, telegram_fallback=fb)\n\n        ok = await w._send_to_telegram({\"summary\": \"hi\"}, \"session-1\")\n\n        assert ok is True\n        assert sent[0][0] == 4242  # the injected fallback delivered\n\n\n# --- lifespan supervision, liveness, interval (adc-4afi) --------------------\n\n\nclass _TaskCrash(BaseException):\n    \"\"\"A task-killing crash.\n\n    Subclasses BaseException (not Exception) so it escapes the watch loop's\n    transient ``except Exception`` catch -- simulating a real task death that\n    only the supervisor layer can recover from. Normal exceptions raised from\n    ``_check_for_events`` are caught by the loop and never reach the supervisor.\n    \"\"\"\n\n\nclass TestInterval:\n    \"\"\"Poll interval defaults to 30s (plan §10) and is overridable.\n\n    Resolution order: explicit constructor arg > ADC_WATCHER_CHECK_INTERVAL_SECONDS\n    env > class default (30s).\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def test_default_interval_is_30(self, store, router, monkeypatch):\n        # Guard against a stray env var in the test environment.\n        monkeypatch.delenv(\"ADC_WATCHER_CHECK_INTERVAL_SECONDS\", raising=False)\n        assert BeadWatcher.CHECK_INTERVAL_SECONDS == 30\n        w = BeadWatcher(store, router)\n        assert w.check_interval_seconds == 30.0\n\n    def test_constructor_arg_overrides_default(self, store, router):\n        w = BeadWatcher(store, router, check_interval_seconds=7)\n        assert w.check_interval_seconds == 7.0\n\n    def test_env_overrides_default_when_no_arg(self, store, router, monkeypatch):\n        monkeypatch.setenv(\"ADC_WATCHER_CHECK_INTERVAL_SECONDS\", \"12\")\n        w = BeadWatcher(store, router)\n        assert w.check_interval_seconds == 12.0\n\n    def test_explicit_arg_beats_env(self, store, router, monkeypatch):\n        monkeypatch.setenv(\"ADC_WATCHER_CHECK_INTERVAL_SECONDS\", \"12\")\n        w = BeadWatcher(store, router, check_interval_seconds=9)\n        assert w.check_interval_seconds == 9.0\n\n\nclass TestLiveness:\n    \"\"\"last_tick_at / tick_count advance on every tick under normal run.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_initial_liveness_state(self, store, router):\n        w = BeadWatcher(store, router)\n        assert w.last_tick_at == 0.0  # 0.0 == \"never ticked\" sentinel\n        assert w.tick_count == 0\n        assert w._close_highwater is None  # HWM starts unbaselined\n\n    async def test_loop_stamps_last_tick_at_and_increments_tick_count(\n        self, store, router, monkeypatch\n    ):\n        w = BeadWatcher(store, router, check_interval_seconds=0.0)\n        # No real subprocess: keep the tick hermetic and empty.\n        async def no_records():\n            return []\n\n        w._run_bf_list_closed = no_records\n\n        # Patch asyncio.sleep in the daemon module: the loop's interval sleep\n        # (seconds < 1.0, since we set the interval to 0.0) becomes instant and\n        # terminates the task after 3 completed ticks, so the test is\n        # deterministic and never waits real time.\n        real_sleep = asyncio.sleep\n        interval_sleeps = {\"n\": 0}\n\n        async def fast_sleep(seconds):\n            await real_sleep(0)  # yield to the scheduler\n            if seconds < 1.0:\n                interval_sleeps[\"n\"] += 1\n                if interval_sleeps[\"n\"] >= 3:\n                    raise asyncio.CancelledError()  # end the watch task\n\n        monkeypatch.setattr(\"src.watcher.daemon.asyncio.sleep\", fast_sleep)\n\n        await w.start()\n        for _ in range(200):\n            if w._supervisor_task is None or w._supervisor_task.done():\n                break\n            await real_sleep(0.002)\n        await w.stop()\n\n        # Each completed tick stamped liveness and bumped the counter.\n        assert w.tick_count >= 3\n        assert w.last_tick_at > 0.0\n\n\nclass TestSupervisorRestart:\n    \"\"\"A crashed watch task is restarted by the supervisor with growing backoff.\n\n    adc-4afi: the old bare create_task loop caught per-iteration exceptions but\n    never restarted a task that ended -- once dead it stayed dead. The supervisor\n    layer now respawns it on death with exponential backoff (2s, 4s, 8s, capped).\n    \"\"\"\n\n    async def test_crashed_task_restarts_with_increasing_backoff(\n        self, store, router, monkeypatch, caplog\n    ):\n        w = BeadWatcher(store, router, check_interval_seconds=0.0)\n\n        # Every tick crashes the task outright (escapes the transient catch).\n        async def crash():\n            raise _TaskCrash(\"forced crash\")\n\n        monkeypatch.setattr(w, \"_check_for_events\", crash)\n\n        # Capture the backoff durations the supervisor requests, without really\n        # sleeping. After the 3rd backoff sleep, stop the supervisor so the test\n        # terminates deterministically.\n        real_sleep = asyncio.sleep\n        requested = []\n\n        async def capture_sleep(seconds):\n            requested.append(seconds)\n            await real_sleep(0)\n            if seconds >= 1.0 and sum(1 for s in requested if s >= 1.0) >= 3:\n                raise asyncio.CancelledError()\n\n        monkeypatch.setattr(\"src.watcher.daemon.asyncio.sleep\", capture_sleep)\n\n        with caplog.at_level(logging.WARNING, logger=\"src.watcher.daemon\"):\n            await w.start()\n            for _ in range(200):\n                if w._supervisor_task is None or w._supervisor_task.done():\n                    break\n                await real_sleep(0.002)\n            await w.stop()\n\n        backoffs = [s for s in requested if s >= 1.0]\n        # Three crashes, each followed by a logged restart with growing backoff.\n        assert len(backoffs) >= 3\n        assert backoffs[0] == pytest.approx(2.0)\n        assert backoffs[1] == pytest.approx(4.0)\n        assert backoffs[2] == pytest.approx(8.0)\n        assert w._restart_count >= 3\n        restart_logs = [r for r in caplog.records if \"restarting\" in r.getMessage().lower()]\n        assert len(restart_logs) >= 3\n\n    async def test_transient_exception_does_not_trigger_restart(\n        self, store, router, monkeypatch\n    ):\n        \"\"\"A normal Exception is caught by the loop -- no supervisor restart.\n\n        Pins the two-layer split: transient per-iteration errors stay absorbed\n        by the watch loop (pre-supervisor behavior preserved); only a real task\n        death reaches the supervisor. So no backoff is ever requested and the\n        task keeps ticking despite the recurring error.\n        \"\"\"\n        w = BeadWatcher(store, router, check_interval_seconds=0.0)\n\n        async def transient():\n            raise RuntimeError(\"transient per-iteration error\")\n\n        monkeypatch.setattr(w, \"_check_for_events\", transient)\n\n        real_sleep = asyncio.sleep\n        sleeps = []\n\n        async def fast_sleep(seconds):\n            sleeps.append(seconds)\n            await real_sleep(0)\n            if seconds < 1.0 and len([s for s in sleeps if s < 1.0]) >= 4:\n                raise asyncio.CancelledError()\n\n        monkeypatch.setattr(\"src.watcher.daemon.asyncio.sleep\", fast_sleep)\n\n        await w.start()\n        for _ in range(200):\n            if w._supervisor_task is None or w._supervisor_task.done():\n                break\n            await real_sleep(0.002)\n        await w.stop()\n\n        # No backoff (>= 1.0) sleep was ever requested, and the task never died.\n        assert not any(s >= 1.0 for s in sleeps)\n        assert w._restart_count == 0\n        # The loop kept ticking -- liveness advanced despite the errors.\n        assert w.tick_count >= 4\n\n\n# --- real bf end-to-end (skip if bf absent) (adc-qw85) ----------------------\n\n\n@pytest.fixture\ndef bf_workspace(tmp_path):\n    \"\"\"An isolated, initialized bead-forge workspace for e2e detection.\"\"\"\n    if not shutil.which(\"bf\"):\n        pytest.skip(\"bf (bead-forge) CLI not on PATH\")\n    ws = tmp_path / \"ws\"\n    ws.mkdir()\n    try:\n        subprocess.run(\n            [\"bf\", \"init\", \"-w\", str(ws)], check=True, capture_output=True, timeout=20\n        )\n    except (subprocess.CalledProcessError, subprocess.TimeoutExpired) as e:\n        pytest.skip(f\"could not bf init a scratch workspace: {e!r}\")\n    return ws\n\n\ndef _bf_run(ws, *args):\n    \"\"\"Run a bf subcommand in workspace ``ws`` and return its stdout.\"\"\"\n    return subprocess.run(\n        [\"bf\", *args, \"-w\", str(ws)],\n        check=True,\n        capture_output=True,\n        text=True,\n        timeout=20,\n    ).stdout\n\n\nclass TestRealBfEndToEnd:\n    \"\"\"Exercises the real CLI + parser. Pins acceptance: a closure is detected\n    within one tick, and a restart does not re-deliver the backlog. Skipped when\n    bf (bead-forge) is unavailable, so the suite stays hermetic elsewhere.\"\"\"\n\n    async def test_detects_new_closure_within_one_tick(self, store, router, bf_workspace):\n        ws = bf_workspace\n        # Pre-existing closed bead (the backlog at watcher start).\n        pre = _bf_run(ws, \"create\", \"--type\", \"task\", \"--title\", \"pre\").strip().splitlines()[0]\n        _bf_run(ws, \"close\", pre)\n\n        w = BeadWatcher(store, router, bf_workspace=str(ws))\n        # First tick: baseline against the backlog, emit nothing.\n        assert await w._poll_closed_beads() == []\n\n        # A bead closes after the watcher started.\n        fresh = _bf_run(ws, \"create\", \"--type\", \"task\", \"--title\", \"fresh\").strip().splitlines()[0]\n        _bf_run(ws, \"close\", fresh)\n\n        # Next tick must detect the new closure and carry its close time.\n        ev = await w._poll_closed_beads()\n        assert [e.bead_id for e in ev] == [fresh]\n        assert ev[0].timestamp > 0\n        assert ev[0].event_type == \"closed\"\n\n    async def test_restart_does_not_redeliver(self, store, router, bf_workspace):\n        ws = bf_workspace\n        bid = _bf_run(ws, \"create\", \"--type\", \"task\", \"--title\", \"closed-before-start\").strip().splitlines()[0]\n        _bf_run(ws, \"close\", bid)\n\n        w = BeadWatcher(store, router, bf_workspace=str(ws))\n        assert await w._poll_closed_beads() == []  # baseline\n\n        # New instance (restart): in-memory HWM lost -> re-baseline, no re-deliver.\n        w2 = BeadWatcher(store, router, bf_workspace=str(ws))\n        assert await w2._poll_closed_beads() == []\n","numLines":919,"startLine":1,"totalLines":919}}}

after N refusals\n255\tCIRCUIT_BREAKER_AGE_THRESHOLD_HOURS = 24.0  # Fence after N hours without progress\n256\t\n257\t\n258\tclass SessionStore:\n259\t    \"\"\"Session store with WAL mode for
