db.execute(\"PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL\")\n269\t            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL\")\n270\t\n271\t            # Create schema\n272\t            await db.executescript(SCHEMA_SQL)\n273\t            await db.commit()\n274\t\n275\t            # Additive migrations: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS above creates new\n276\t            # columns only for freshly-made DBs; an existing data/session.db\n277\t            # (e.g. the live Hetzner box) keeps its old column set and must be\n278\t            # ALTERed. Each statement is guarded by a PRAGMA table_info probe so\n279\t            # it is idempotent and never re-runs once the column exists.\n280\t            await self._migrate_additive_columns(db)\n281\t\n282\t    @staticmethod\n283\t    async def _migrate_additive_columns(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n284\t        \"\"\"Idempotently add columns introduced after the initial schema.\"\"\"\n285\t        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(results)\") as cur:\n286\t            result_cols = {row[1] for row in await cur.fetchall()}\n287\t        if \"result_type\" not in result_cols:\n288\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE results ADD COLUMN result_type TEXT\")\n289\t        if \"card_fallback\" not in result_cols:\n290\t            await db.execute(\n291\t                \"ALTER TABLE results ADD COLUMN card_fallback INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0\"\n292\t            )\n293\t\n294\t        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(intents)\") as cur:\n295\t            intent_cols = {row[1] for row in await cur.fetchall()}\n296\t        if \"lookup_kind\" not in intent_cols:\n297\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE intents ADD COLUMN lookup_kind TEXT\")\n298\t\n299\t        # Migrate intents.status CHECK constraint to include 'stuck' and 'failed'\n300\t        # SQLite doesn't support ALTER CONSTRAINT, so we need to recreate the table\n301\t        # First, check if the table still has the old constraint (without stuck/failed)\n302\t        await SessionStore._migrate_intents_status_enum(db)\n303\t\n304\t        # Migrate results.intent_id to allow NULL for monitoring-originated results\n305\t        await SessionStore._migrate_results_intent_id_nullable(db)\n306\t\n307\t        await db.commit()\n308\t\n309\t    @staticmethod\n310\t    async def _migrate_intents_status_enum(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n311\t        \"\"\"Migrate intents table to support 'stuck' and 'failed' status values.\n312\t\n313\t        Plan §10 The Async Path: extend intents status enum with stuck/failed.\n314\t        SQLite doesn't support ALTER CONSTRAINT, so we recreate the table.\n315\t        \"\"\"\n316\t        # Check if migration is needed by looking for 'stuck' in the schema\n317\t        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(intents)\") as cur:\n318\t            cols = await cur.fetchall()\n319\t            status_col = None\n320\t            for col in cols:\n321\t                if col[1] == \"status\":\n322\t                    status_col = col\n323\t                    break\n324\t\n325\t        # If no status column or already has stuck/failed, skip migration\n326\t        if not status_col:\n327\t            return\n328\t\n329\t        # Check if the CHECK constraint already includes stuck/failed\n330\t        # by inspecting the SQL for the intents table\n331\t        async with db.execute(\n332\t            \"SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='intents'\"\n333\t        ) as cur:\n334\t            table_sql = await cur.fetchone()\n335\t            if table_sql and (\"'stuck'\" in table_sql[0] or \"'failed'\" in table_sql[0]):\n336\t                # Already migrated\n337\t                return\n338\t\n339\t        # Migration needed: recreate table with new constraint\n340\t        logger.info(\"Migrating intents.status to include 'stuck' and 'failed' values\")\n341\t\n342\t        # Begin transaction for data migration\n343\t        await db.execute(\"BEGIN IMMEDIATE TRANSACTION\")\n344\t\n345\t        try:\n346\t            # Create new table with updated constraint\n347\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n348\t                CREATE TABLE intents_new (\n349\t                    id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n350\t                    utterance_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n351\t                    session_id   TEXT NOT NULL,\n352\t                    topic_id     TEXT,\n353\t                    project_slug TEXT,\n354\t                    intent_type  TEXT NOT NULL,\n355\t                    lookup_kind  TEXT,\n356\t                    status       TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(status IN ('pending', 'dispatched', 'resolved', 'cancelled', 'stuck', 'failed')) DEFAULT 'pending',\n357\t                    bead_ref     TEXT,\n358\t                    created_at   INTEGER NOT NULL,\n359\t                    resolved_at  INTEGER,\n360\t                    FOREIGN KEY (utterance_id) REFERENCES utterances(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n361\t                    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n362\t                    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n363\t                )\n364\t            \"\"\")\n365\t\n366\t            # Copy data from old table to new table\n367\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n368\t                INSERT INTO intents_new\n369\t                SELECT id, utterance_id, session_id, topic_id, project_slug,\n370\t                       intent_type, lookup_kind, status, bead_ref, created_at, resolved_at\n371\t                FROM intents\n372\t            \"\"\")\n373\t\n374\t            # Recreate indexes\n375\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_intents_session\")\n376\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_intents_topic\")\n377\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_intents_status\")\n378\t\n379\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_intents_session ON intents_new(session_id)\")\n380\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_intents_topic ON intents_new(topic_id)\")\n381\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_intents_status ON intents_new(status)\")\n382\t\n383\t            # Drop old table and rename new table\n384\t            await db.execute(\"DROP TABLE intents\")\n385\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE intents_new RENAME TO intents\")\n386\t\n387\t            await db.commit()\n388\t            logger.info(\"Migration complete: intents.status now supports 'stuck' and 'failed'\")\n389\t\n390\t        except Exception as e:\n391\t            await db.rollback()\n392\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to migrate intents.status: {e}\")\n393\t            raise\n394\t\n395\t    @staticmethod\n396\t    async def _migrate_results_intent_id_nullable(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n397\t        \"\"\"Migrate results table to allow NULL intent_id for monitoring-originated results.\n398\t\n399\t        Plan §10 Bead Watcher: monitoring-originated results have intent_id=NULL.\n400\t        SQLite doesn't support ALTER COLUMN to drop NOT NULL, so we recreate the table.\n401\t        \"\"\"\n402\t        # Check if migration is needed by inspecting the results table schema\n403\t        async with db.execute(\n404\t            \"SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='results'\"\n405\t        ) as cur:\n406\t            table_sql = await cur.fetchone()\n407\t            if not table_sql:\n408\t                return  # Table doesn't exist yet\n409\t\n410\t            # If intent_id is already nullable (NOT NULL not present), skip migration\n411\t            if \"intent_id   TEXT,\" in table_sql[0] or \"intent_id TEXT,\" in table_sql[0]:\n412\t                if \"NOT NULL\" not in table_sql[0].split(\"intent_id\")[1].split(\",\")[0]:\n413\t                    # Already migrated\n414\t                    return\n415\t\n416\t        # Migration needed: recreate table with nullable intent_id\n417\t        logger.info(\"Migrating results.intent_id to allow NULL for monitoring-originated results\")\n418\t\n419\t        # Begin transaction for data migration\n420\t        await db.execute(\"BEGIN IMMEDIATE TRANSACTION\")\n421\t\n422\t        try:\n423\t            # Create new table with nullable intent_id\n424\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n425\t                CREATE TABLE results_new (\n426\t                    id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n427\t                    intent_id   TEXT,\n428\t                    topic_id    TEXT NOT NULL,\n429\t                    session_id  TEXT NOT NULL,\n430\t                    summary     TEXT NOT NULL,\n431\t                    data        TEXT NOT NULL,\n432\t                    urgency     TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(urgency IN ('critical', 'high', 'normal', 'low')) DEFAULT 'normal',\n433\t                    result_type TEXT,\n434\t                    card_fallback INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 CHECK(card_fallback IN (0, 1)),\n435\t                    created_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n436\t                    surfaced_at INTEGER,\n437\t                    acked_at    INTEGER,\n438\t                    previous_result_id TEXT,\n439\t                    diff_summary TEXT,\n440\t                    diff_data    TEXT,\n441\t                    FOREIGN KEY (intent_id) REFERENCES intents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n442\t                    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n443\t                    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n444\t                    FOREIGN KEY (previous_result_id) REFERENCES results(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n445\t                )\n446\t            \"\"\")\n447\t\n448\t            # Copy data from old table to new table\n449\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n450\t                INSERT INTO results_new\n451\t                SELECT * FROM results\n452\t            \"\"\")\n453\t\n454\t            # Recreate indexes\n455\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_results_session\")\n456\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_results_topic\")\n457\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_results_created\")\n458\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_results_previous\")\n459\t\n460\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_results_session ON results_new(session_id)\")\n461\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_results_topic ON results_new(topic_id)\")\n462\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_results_created ON results_new(created_at)\")\n463\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_results_previous ON results_new(previous_result_id)\")\n464\t\n465\t            # Drop old table and rename new table\n466\t            await db.execute(\"DROP TABLE results\")\n467\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE results_new RENAME TO results\")\n468\t\n469\t            await db.commit()\n470\t            logger.info(\"Migration complete: results.intent_id now allows NULL\")\n471\t        except Exception as e:\n472\t            await db.rollback()\n473\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to migrate results.intent_id: {e}\")\n474\t            raise\n475\t\n476\t    async def close(self) -> None:\n477\t        \"\"\"Close database connection pool.\"\"\"\n478\t        # aiosqlite uses connection-per-context, so just ensure checkpoint\n479\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n480\t            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)\")\n481\t\n482\t    # Session operations\n483\t    async def create_session(self, session_id: str | None = None) -> str:\n484\t        \"\"\"Create a new session and return its ID.\"\"\"\n485\t        if not session_id:\n486\t            session_id = str(uuid4())\n487\t        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n488\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n489\t            await db.execute(\n490\t                \"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO sessions (id, created_at, last_active) VALUES (?, ?, ?)\",\n491\t                (session_id, now, now)\n492\t            )\n493\t            await db.commit()\n494\t        return session_id\n495\t\n496\t    async def get_session(self, session_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n497\t        \"\"\"Get session by ID.\"\"\"\n498\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n499\t            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n500\t            async with db.execute(\n501\t                \"SELECT * FROM sessions WHERE id = ?\", (session_id,)\n502\t            ) as cursor:\n503\t                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n504\t                return dict(row) if row else None\n505\t\n506\t    async def update_session_activity(self, session_id: str) -> None:\n507\t        \"\"\"Update session last_active timestamp.\"\"\"\n508\t        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n509\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n510\t            await db.execute(\n511\t                \"UPDATE sessions SET last_active = ? Returns a removal summary.\n527\t\n528\t        Used by test teardown (DELETE /api/v1/sessions/{id}) to guarantee a\n529\t        session and all its topics/results/intents/utterances/surfaces/signal\n530\t        rows are gone. Note: SQLite FK ``ON DELETE CASCADE`` is *not* enforced\n531\t        here (``PRAGMA foreign_keys`` is never enabled), so child rows are\n532\t        deleted explicitly, in dependency order, within one transaction.\n533\t\n534\t        The topics table references sessions with ``ON DELETE SET NULL``, so\n535\t        topics are *not* cascade-removed even if FKs were on — deleting them\n536\t        explicitly is required for a true clean teardown.\n537\t\n538\t        Returns ``{\"session_removed\": 0|1, \"topics_removed\": <int>}``.\n539\t        \"\"\"\n540\t        async with cur.fetchone())[0]\n548\t            async with db.execute(\n549\t                \"SELECT 1 FROM sessions WHERE id = ?\", (session_id,)\n550\t            ) as cur:\n551\t                session_removed = 1 if await cur.fetchone() else 0\n552\t\n553\t            # Children first, parents last (matches FK dependency order).\n554\t            await db.execute(\n555\t (session_id,)\n559\t            )\n560\t            await db.execute(\n561\t                \"DELETE FROM intent_topics WHERE intent_id IN \"\n562\t                \"(SELECT id FROM intents WHERE \"DELETE FROM intents WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n577\t            )\n578\t            await db.execute(\n579\t                \"DELETE FROM utterances WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n580\t            )\n581\t            await db.execute(\n582\t                \"DELETE FROM topic_context_cache WHERE topic_id IN \"\n583\t                \"(SELECT id FROM topics WHERE           )\n589\t            await db.execute(\n590\t                \"DELETE FROM surfaces WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n591\t            )\n592\t            await db.execute(\n593\t                \"DELETE FROM sessions WHERE id = ?\", (session_id,)\n594\t            )\n595\t            await db.commit()\n596\t\n597\t        return {\"session_removed\": session_removed, \"topics_removed\": topics_removed}\n598\t\n599\t    # Surface operations\n600\t    async def register_surface(\n601\t        self,\n602\t        session_id: str,\n603\t        surface_type: Ensure the row exists (created_at set once, on first write).\n1151\t            await db.execute(\n1152\t                \"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO dispatch_timings (intent_id, created_at) VALUES (?, ?)\",\n1153\t                (intent_id, now),\n1154\t            )\n1155\t            if cols:\n1156\t                set_clause = \", \".join(f\"{c} = ?\" for c in cols)\n1157\t                params = [*cols.values(), intent_id]\n1158\t                await db.execute(\n1159\t                    f\"UPDATE dispatch_timings SET {set_clause} WHERE intent_id = ?\",\n1160\t                    params,\n1161\t                )\n1162\t            await db.commit()\n1163\t\n1164\t    async def get_dispatch_timings(self, intent_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n1165\t        \"\"\"Get the dispatch_timings row for one intent thread, or None.\"\"\"\n1166\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n1167\t            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n1168\t            async with db.execute(\n1169\t                \"SELECT * FROM dispatch_timings WHERE intent_id = ?\",\n1170\t                (intent_id,),\n1171\t            ) as cursor:\n1172\t                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n1173\t                return dict(row) if row else None\n1174\t\n1175\t    async def get_latency_percentiles(self, since: int | None = None) -> dict:\n1176\t        \"\"\"Aggregate p50/p95 per stage across dispatch_timings rows.\n1177\t\n1178\t        Returns ``{stage: {\"p50\": ms, \"p95\": ms, \"count\": n}}`` for every stage\n1179\t        that has at least one non-NULL value. NULLs are skipped per stage (so a\n1180\t        stage not yet captured for any dispatch simply doesn't appear). Pass\n1181\t        ``since`` (a unix timestamp) to window to recent dispatches only — the\n1182\t        latency-baseline bead consumes the un-windowed call to fill the plan's\n1183\t        global Measured p50/p95 columns.\n1184\t\n1185\t        Percentiles are nearest-rank (ceil(q/100 * n)), computed in Python\n1186\t        because SQLite has no built-in percentile.\n1187\t        \"\"\"\n1188\t        from ..instrument.timings import percentiles\n1189\t\n1190\t        result: dict[str, dict] = {}\n1191\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n1192\t            for stage in DISPATCH_TIMING_COLUMNS:\n1193\t                if since is not None:\n1194\t                    query = (\n1195\t                        f\"SELECT {stage} FROM dispatch_timings \"\n1196\t                        f\"WHERE {stage} IS NOT NULL AND created_at >= ? \"\n1197\t                        f\"ORDER BY {stage}\"\n1198\t                    )\n1199\t                    params: tuple = (since,)\n1200\t                else:\n1201\t                    query = (\n1202\t                        f\"SELECT {stage} FROM dispatch_timings \"\n1203\t                        f\"WHERE {stage} IS NOT NULL ORDER BY {stage}\"\n1204\t                    )\n1205\t                    params = ()\n1206\t                async with db.execute(query, params) as cursor:\n1207\t                    values = [row[0] for row in await cursor.fetchall()]\n1208\t                if not values:\n1209\t                    continue\n1210\t                pct = percentiles(values, qs=(50, 95))\n1211\t                result[stage] = {\n1212\t                    \"p50\": pct[50],\n1213\t                    \"p95\": pct[95],\n1214\t                    \"count\": len(values),\n1215\t                }\n1216\t        return result\n1217\t\n1218\t    async def get_session_feedback_summary(\n1219\t        self,\n1220\t        session_id: str,\n1221\t        since: int | None = None,\n1222\t    ) -> dict:\n1223\t        \"\"\"Get a summary of feedback signals for a session.\"\"\"\n1224\t        one_hour_ago = int(datetime.now().timestamp()) - 3600\n1225\t\n1226\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n1227\t            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n1228\t\n1229\t            # Get signal counts by type\n1230\t            since_clause = since or one_hour_ago\n1231\t            async with db.execute(\n1232\t                \"\"\"SELECT signal_type, COUNT(*) as count\n1233\t                   FROM feedback_signals\n1234\t                   WHERE session_id = ? AND timestamp >= ?\n1235\t                   GROUP BY signal_type\"\"\",\n1236\t                (session_id, since_clause)\n1237\t            ) as cursor:\n1238\t                type_counts = {row[\"signal_type\"]: row[\"count\"] for row in await cursor.fetchall()}\n1239\t\n1240\t            # Get average ack delay\n1241\t            async with db.execute(\n1242\t                \"\"\"SELECT AVG(CAST(data->>'ack_delay_seconds' AS INTEGER)) as avg_delay\n1243\t                   FROM feedback_signals\n1244\t                   WHERE session_id = ? \"avg_ack_delay_seconds\": avg_ack_delay,\n1253\t            }\n1254\t\n1255\t    async def create_result_with_diff(\n1256\t        self,\n1257\t        intent_id: str,\n1258\t        topic_id: str,\n1259\t        session_id: str,\n1260\t        summary: str,\n1261\t        data: dict,\n1262\t        urgency: str = \"normal\",\n1263\t    ) -> tuple[str, bool]:\n1264\t        \"\"\"\n1265\t        Create a new result with automatic diff computation.\n1266\t\n1267\t        Returns (result_id, has_diff).\n1268\t\n1269\t        Convenience method that automatically computes the diff against\n1270\t        the previous result for the same topic (if one exists).\n1271\t        \"\"\"\n1272\t        from ..diff.engine import get_diff_engine\n1273\t\n1274\t        # Get previous     )\n1289\t\n1290\t            if diff_result.has_changes:\n1291\t                has_diff = True\n1292\t                diff_summary = diff_result.change_summary\n1293\t                diff_data =       for f in diff_result.fields\n1302\t                    ],\n1303\t                    \"summary\": diff_result.summary,\n1304\t                }\n1305\t\n1306\t        # Create the result with Overridable via ADC_DB_PATH env var so tests can point at an\n# isolated temp/in-memory DB instead of the production data/session.db.\nDEFAULT_DB_PATH = Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/data/session.db\")\n\n# Schema definition\nSCHEMA_SQL = \"\"\"\n-- Sessions: surface-agnostic persistent entities\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sessions (\n    id              TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n    created_at      INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    last_active     INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    primary_surface_id TEXT\n);\n\n-- Surfaces: transient windows into sessions\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS surfaces (\n    id              TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n    session_id      TEXT NOT NULL,\n    type            TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(type IN ('canvas', 'telegram', 'audio')),\n    state           TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(state IN ('active', 'idle', 'disconnected')) DEFAULT 'active',\n    always_available INTEGER DEFAULT 0 CHECK(always_available IN (0, 1)),\n    last_seen       INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n);\n\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_surfaces_session ON surfaces(session_id);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_surfaces_state ON surfaces(state);\n\n-- Utterances: raw input from user\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS utterances (\n    id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n    session_id  TEXT NOT NULL,\n    raw_text    TEXT NOT NULL,\n    created_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n);\n\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_utterances_session ON utterances(session_id);\n\n-- Intents: parsed intent threads from utterances\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS intents (\n    id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n    utterance_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n    session_id   TEXT NOT NULL,\n    topic_id     TEXT,\n    project_slug TEXT,\n    intent_type  TEXT NOT NULL,\n    lookup_kind  TEXT,  -- lookup intents only: 'logs' | 'config' | 'docs' (router-emitted; see Intent Router). NULL otherwise\n    status       TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(status IN ('pending', 'dispatched', 'resolved', 'cancelled')) DEFAULT 'pending',\n    bead_ref     TEXT,\n    created_at   INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    resolved_at  INTEGER,\n    FOREIGN KEY (utterance_id) REFERENCES utterances(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n);\n\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_intents_session ON intents(session_id);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_intents_topic ON intents(topic_id);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_intents_status ON intents(status);\n\n-- Results: structured data returned by agents\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS results (\n    id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n    intent_id   TEXT,  -- NULL for monitoring-originated results (system-originated, no utterance)\n    topic_id    TEXT NOT NULL,\n    session_id  TEXT NOT NULL,\n    summary     TEXT NOT NULL,\n    data        TEXT NOT NULL,  -- JSON\n    urgency     TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(urgency IN ('critical', 'high', 'normal', 'low')) DEFAULT 'normal',\n    result_type TEXT,  -- deterministic card-selector key, set at result-write time:\n                       -- \"{intent_type}:{project_slug}\" for intent-derived results — one per\n                       -- intent thread (the aggregated thread card); lookup threads insert the\n                       -- intent's lookup_kind: \"lookup:{lookup_kind}:{project_slug}\";\n                       -- \"monitoring:{project_slug}\" for monitoring-originated rows. The hot-path\n                       -- component lookup keys on this column, no LLM (see UI-Regen Agent /\n                       -- component_usage_patterns).\n    card_fallback INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 CHECK(card_fallback IN (0, 1)),  -- 1 when no component matched this result_type (or below threshold): the client renders the built-in generic fallback card (see Component Library → Built-in generic fallback card). 0 when a real component rendered it (card_cache holds the rendered HTML).\n    created_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    surfaced_at INTEGER,\n    acked_at    INTEGER,\n    previous_result_id TEXT,  -- Link to previous result for diff\n    diff_summary TEXT,  -- Human-readable diff summary\n    diff_data    TEXT,  -- JSON: detailed field diffs\n    FOREIGN KEY (intent_id) REFERENCES intents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n    FOREIGN KEY (previous_result_id) REFERENCES results(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n);\n\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_results_session ON results(session_id);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_results_topic ON results(topic_id);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_results_created ON results(created_at);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_results_previous ON results(previous_result_id);\n\n-- Topics: persistent concerns organizing intents and results\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS topics (\n    id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n    label        TEXT NOT NULL,\n    type         TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(type IN ('project', 'research', 'personal', 'exception', 'compound')) DEFAULT 'adhoc',\n    project_slugs TEXT,  -- JSON array\n    scope        TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(scope IN ('session', 'cross-session', 'global')) DEFAULT 'session',\n    session_id   TEXT,\n    created_at   INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    last_active  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    archived_at  INTEGER,\n    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n);\n\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_topics_session ON topics(session_id);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_topics_scope ON topics(scope);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_topics_active ON topics(last_active DESC);\n\n-- Topic context cache: pre-warmed context for active topics\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS topic_context_cache (\n    topic_id     TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n    context_data TEXT NOT NULL,  -- JSON: pre-fetched context (kubectl, git, beads results)\n    fetched_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    expires_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n);\n\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_context_expires ON topic_context_cache(expires_at);\n\n-- Intent-topic many-to-many relationship\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS intent_topics (\n    intent_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n    topic_id  TEXT NOT NULL,\n    PRIMARY KEY (intent_id, topic_id),\n    FOREIGN KEY (intent_id) REFERENCES intents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n);\n\n-- Feedback signals: implicit user behavior tracking for background analysis\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS feedback_signals (\n    signal_id    TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n    signal_type  TEXT NOT NULL,\n    session_id   TEXT NOT NULL,\n    result_id    TEXT,\n    topic_id     TEXT,\n    timestamp    INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    data         TEXT NOT NULL,  -- JSON: signal-specific data\n    surface_type TEXT,\n    processed    INTEGER DEFAULT 0 CHECK(processed IN (0, 1)),\n    processed_at INTEGER,\n    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n    FOREIGN KEY (result_id) REFERENCES results(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,\n    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n);\n\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_session ON feedback_signals(session_id);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_type ON feedback_signals(signal_type);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_processed ON feedback_signals(processed);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_result ON feedback_signals(result_id);\n\n-- Per-stage latency capture for every dispatch (Latency Budget & Instrumentation).\n-- Keyed by the intent *thread* id (routed_intent.intent_id — the same id the\n-- fetch/synthesize/escalate strands and the results row use), NOT the\n-- intents.id the caller mints in create_intent(). router_ms is shared across\n-- every intent thread from the same utterance; escalate_ms is NULL for hot-path\n-- dispatches; stt_ms/first_render_ms are client-reported and NULL when the\n-- client never reports them. synthesize_first_token_ms is NULL until the\n-- synthesize strand streams (the non-streaming call_simple path can't measure\n-- first token separately) — see src/instrument/timings.py.\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS dispatch_timings (\n    intent_id                 TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n    router_ms                 INTEGER,\n    fetch_first_source_ms     INTEGER,\n    fetch_total_ms            INTEGER,\n    synthesize_first_token_ms INTEGER,\n    synthesize_total_ms       INTEGER,\n    escalate_ms               INTEGER,\n    sse_emit_ms               INTEGER,\n    stt_ms                    INTEGER,\n    first_render_ms           INTEGER,\n    created_at                INTEGER NOT NULL\n);\n\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dispatch_timings_created ON dispatch_timings(created_at);\n\n-- Bead watch table: circuit breaker tracking for async beads\n-- Persistence layer for the async-path circuit breaker (plan §10 The Async Path).\n-- Tracks open beads watched by the BeadWatcher, recording refusal counts,\n-- last refusal reasons, SLA deadlines, and fencing state. All state is persisted\n-- so a watcher restart loses nothing -- breaker state lives in the database,\n-- not only in memory.\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS bead_watch (\n    bead_ref           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,  -- References bead ID (intents.bead_ref)\n    refusal_count      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,  -- Number of REFUSED: comments seen\n    last_refusal_reason TEXT,  -- Most recent refusal reason\n    last_refusal_at    INTEGER,  -- Timestamp of most recent refusal\n    comment_high_water INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,  -- Latest comment index processed\n    sla_deadline       INTEGER NOT NULL,  -- Unix timestamp when SLA expires\n    sla_flagged_at     INTEGER,  -- Timestamp when SLA was flagged (NULL if not flagged)\n    fenced_at          INTEGER,  -- Timestamp when bead was fenced to status=blocked (NULL if not fenced)\n    created_at         INTEGER NOT NULL  -- When this watch row was created\n);\n\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_bead_watch_sla_deadline ON bead_watch(sla_deadline);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_bead_watch_fenced ON bead_watch(fenced_at);\n\"\"\"\n\n# The set of dispatch_timings timing columns record_dispatch_timings() may set.\n# intent_id/created_at are handled explicitly (PK / insert-only timestamp).\nDISPATCH_TIMING_COLUMNS = (\n    \"router_ms\",\n    \"fetch_first_source_ms\",\n    \"fetch_total_ms\",\n    \"synthesize_first_token_ms\",\n    \"synthesize_total_ms\",\n    \"escalate_ms\",\n    \"sse_emit_ms\",\n    \"stt_ms\",\n    \"first_render_ms\",\n)\n\n# Valid intent status values (plan §10 The Async Path: stuck/failed added for circuit breaker)\nINTENT_STATUSES = ('pending', 'dispatched', 'resolved', 'cancelled', 'stuck', 'failed')\n\n# Default SLA hours per intent type (plan §10 The Async Path: Visible Aging)\n# task-profile: 6h (async bead-backed tasks)\n# hot-path intents: 30s (budget is 3s, flag at 10x for aged pending)\nDEFAULT_SLA_HOURS: dict[str, float] = {\n    \"task-profile\": 6.0,  # 6 hours for async bead-backed tasks\n    \"status\": 0.008,     # 30 seconds for hot-path status intents\n    \"action\": 0.008,     # 30 seconds for hot-path action intents\n    \"lookup\": 0.008,     # 30 seconds for hot-path lookup intents\n    \"brainstorm\": 0.5,   # 30 minutes for brainstorm (may need user iteration)\n    \"reminder\": 24.0,    # 24 hours for reminders\n}\n\n# Circuit breaker thresholds (plan §10 The Async Path)\nCIRCUIT_BREAKER_REFUSAL_THRESHOLD = 3  # Fence after N refusals\nCIRCUIT_BREAKER_AGE_THRESHOLD_HOURS = 24.0  # Fence after N hours without progress\n\n\nclass SessionStore:\n    \"\"\"Session store with WAL mode for concurrent access.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, db_path: Path):\n        self.db_path = db_path\n\n    async def initialize(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Initialize database with schema and WAL mode.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            # Enable WAL mode for concurrent reads during writes\n            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL\")\n            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL\")\n\n            # Create schema\n            await db.executescript(SCHEMA_SQL)\n            await db.commit()\n\n            # Additive migrations: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS above creates new\n            # columns only for freshly-made DBs; an existing data/session.db\n            # (e.g. the live Hetzner box) keeps its old column set and must be\n            # ALTERed. Each statement is guarded by a PRAGMA table_info probe so\n            # it is idempotent and never re-runs once the column exists.\n            await self._migrate_additive_columns(db)\n\n    @staticmethod\n    async def _migrate_additive_columns(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Idempotently add columns introduced after the initial schema.\"\"\"\n        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(results)\") as cur:\n            result_cols = {row[1] for row in await cur.fetchall()}\n        if \"result_type\" not in result_cols:\n            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE results ADD COLUMN result_type TEXT\")\n        if \"card_fallback\" not in result_cols:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"ALTER TABLE results ADD COLUMN card_fallback INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0\"\n            )\n\n        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(intents)\") as cur:\n            intent_cols = {row[1] for row in await cur.fetchall()}\n        if \"lookup_kind\" not in intent_cols:\n            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE intents ADD COLUMN lookup_kind TEXT\")\n\n        # Migrate intents.status CHECK constraint to include 'stuck' and 'failed'\n        # SQLite doesn't support ALTER CONSTRAINT, so we need to recreate the table\n        # First, check if the table still has the old constraint (without stuck/failed)\n        await SessionStore._migrate_intents_status_enum(db)\n\n        # Migrate results.intent_id to allow NULL for monitoring-originated results\n        await SessionStore._migrate_results_intent_id_nullable(db)\n\n        await db.commit()\n\n    @staticmethod\n    async def _migrate_intents_status_enum(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Migrate intents table to support 'stuck' and 'failed' status values.\n\n        Plan §10 The Async Path: extend intents status enum with stuck/failed.\n        SQLite doesn't support ALTER CONSTRAINT, so we recreate the table.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Check if migration is needed by looking for 'stuck' in the schema\n        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(intents)\") as cur:\n            cols = await cur.fetchall()\n            status_col = None\n            for col in cols:\n                if col[1] == \"status\":\n                    status_col = col\n                    break\n\n        # If no status column or already has stuck/failed, skip migration\n        if not status_col:\n            return\n\n        # Check if the CHECK constraint already includes stuck/failed\n        # by inspecting the SQL for the intents table\n        async with db.execute(\n            \"SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='intents'\"\n        ) as cur:\n            table_sql = await cur.fetchone()\n            if table_sql and (\"'stuck'\" in table_sql[0] or \"'failed'\" in table_sql[0]):\n                # Already migrated\n                return\n\n        # Migration needed: recreate table with new constraint\n        logger.info(\"Migrating intents.status to include 'stuck' and 'failed' values\")\n\n        # Begin transaction for data migration\n        await db.execute(\"BEGIN IMMEDIATE TRANSACTION\")\n\n        try:\n            # Create new table with updated constraint\n            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n                CREATE TABLE intents_new (\n                    id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n                    utterance_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n                    session_id   TEXT NOT NULL,\n                    topic_id     TEXT,\n                    project_slug TEXT,\n                    intent_type  TEXT NOT NULL,\n                    lookup_kind  TEXT,\n                    status       TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(status IN ('pending', 'dispatched', 'resolved', 'cancelled', 'stuck', 'failed')) DEFAULT 'pending',\n                    bead_ref     TEXT,\n                    created_at   INTEGER NOT NULL,\n                    resolved_at  INTEGER,\n                    FOREIGN KEY (utterance_id) REFERENCES utterances(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n                    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n                    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n                )\n            \"\"\")\n\n            # Copy data from old table to new table\n            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n                INSERT INTO intents_new\n                SELECT id, utterance_id, session_id, topic_id, project_slug,\n                       intent_type, lookup_kind, status, bead_ref, created_at, resolved_at\n                FROM intents\n            \"\"\")\n\n            # Recreate indexes\n            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_intents_session\")\n            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_intents_topic\")\n            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_intents_status\")\n\n            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_intents_session ON intents_new(session_id)\")\n            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_intents_topic ON intents_new(topic_id)\")\n            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_intents_status ON intents_new(status)\")\n\n            # Drop old table and rename new table\n            await db.execute(\"DROP TABLE intents\")\n            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE intents_new RENAME TO intents\")\n\n            await db.commit()\n            logger.info(\"Migration complete: intents.status now supports 'stuck' and 'failed'\")\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            await db.rollback()\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to migrate intents.status: {e}\")\n            raise\n\n    @staticmethod\n    async def _migrate_results_intent_id_nullable(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Migrate results table to allow NULL intent_id for monitoring-originated results.\n\n        Plan §10 Bead Watcher: monitoring-originated results have intent_id=NULL.\n        SQLite doesn't support ALTER COLUMN to drop NOT NULL, so we recreate the table.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Check if migration is needed by inspecting the results table schema\n        async with db.execute(\n            \"SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='results'\"\n        ) as cur:\n            table_sql = await cur.fetchone()\n            if not table_sql:\n                return  # Table doesn't exist yet\n\n            # If intent_id is already nullable (NOT NULL not present), skip migration\n            if \"intent_id   TEXT,\" in table_sql[0] or \"intent_id TEXT,\" in table_sql[0]:\n                if \"NOT NULL\" not in table_sql[0].split(\"intent_id\")[1].split(\",\")[0]:\n                    # Already migrated\n                    return\n\n        # Migration needed: recreate table with nullable intent_id\n        logger.info(\"Migrating results.intent_id to allow NULL for monitoring-originated results\")\n\n        # Begin transaction for data migration\n        await db.execute(\"BEGIN IMMEDIATE TRANSACTION\")\n\n        try:\n            # Create new table with nullable intent_id\n            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n                CREATE TABLE results_new (\n                    id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n                    intent_id   TEXT,\n                    topic_id    TEXT NOT NULL,\n                    session_id  TEXT NOT NULL,\n                    summary     TEXT NOT NULL,\n                    data        TEXT NOT NULL,\n                    urgency     TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(urgency IN ('critical', 'high', 'normal', 'low')) DEFAULT 'normal',\n                    result_type TEXT,\n                    card_fallback INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 CHECK(card_fallback IN (0, 1)),\n                    created_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n                    surfaced_at INTEGER,\n                    acked_at    INTEGER,\n                    previous_result_id TEXT,\n                    diff_summary TEXT,\n                    diff_data    TEXT,\n                    FOREIGN KEY (intent_id) REFERENCES intents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n                    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n                    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n                    FOREIGN KEY (previous_result_id) REFERENCES results(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n                )\n            \"\"\")\n\n            # Copy data from old table to new table\n            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n                INSERT INTO results_new\n                SELECT * FROM results\n            \"\"\")\n\n            # Recreate indexes\n            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_results_session\")\n            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_results_topic\")\n            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_results_created\")\n            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_results_previous\")\n\n            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_results_session ON results_new(session_id)\")\n            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_results_topic ON results_new(topic_id)\")\n            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_results_created ON results_new(created_at)\")\n            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_results_previous ON results_new(previous_result_id)\")\n\n            # Drop old table and rename new table\n            await db.execute(\"DROP TABLE results\")\n            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE results_new RENAME TO results\")\n\n            await db.commit()\n            logger.info(\"Migration complete: results.intent_id now allows NULL\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            await db.rollback()\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to migrate results.intent_id: {e}\")\n            raise\n\n    async def close(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Close database connection pool.\"\"\"\n        # aiosqlite uses connection-per-context, so just ensure checkpoint\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)\")\n\n    # Session operations\n    async def create_session(self, session_id: str | None = None) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Create a new session and return its ID.\"\"\"\n        if not session_id:\n            session_id = str(uuid4())\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO sessions (id, created_at, last_active) VALUES (?, ?, ?)\",\n                (session_id, now, now)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n        return session_id\n\n    async def get_session(self, session_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get session by ID.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"SELECT * FROM sessions WHERE id = ?\", (session_id,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                return dict(row) if row else None\n\n    async def update_session_activity(self, session_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Update session last_active timestamp.\"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"UPDATE sessions SET last_active = ? WHERE id = ?\",\n                (now, session_id)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def set_primary_surface(self, session_id: str, surface_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Set the primary surface for a session.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"UPDATE sessions SET primary_surface_id = ? WHERE id = ?\",\n                (surface_id, session_id)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def delete_session(self, session_id: str) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Delete a session and every row tied to it. Returns a removal summary.\n\n        Used by test teardown (DELETE /api/v1/sessions/{id}) to guarantee a\n        session and all its topics/results/intents/utterances/surfaces/signal\n        rows are gone. Note: SQLite FK ``ON DELETE CASCADE`` is *not* enforced\n        here (``PRAGMA foreign_keys`` is never enabled), so child rows are\n        deleted explicitly, in dependency order, within one transaction.\n\n        The topics table references sessions with ``ON DELETE SET NULL``, so\n        topics are *not* cascade-removed even if FKs were on — deleting them\n        explicitly is required for a true clean teardown.\n\n        Returns ``{\"session_removed\": 0|1, \"topics_removed\": <int>}``.\n        \"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n\n            # Count what we're about to remove (before deleting).\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM topics WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n            ) as cur:\n                topics_removed = (await cur.fetchone())[0]\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"SELECT 1 FROM sessions WHERE id = ?\", (session_id,)\n            ) as cur:\n                session_removed = 1 if await cur.fetchone() else 0\n\n            # Children first, parents last (matches FK dependency order).\n            await db.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM feedback_signals WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n            )\n            await db.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM results WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n            )\n            await db.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM intent_topics WHERE intent_id IN \"\n                \"(SELECT id FROM intents WHERE session_id = ?)\",\n                (session_id,),\n            )\n            # dispatch_timings is keyed by the intent *thread* id\n            # (routed_intent.intent_id), which differs from intents.id for the\n            # current router path (see the table comment in SCHEMA_SQL), so this\n            # is best-effort: it catches rows keyed by the store intent id, while\n            # thread-keyd rows survive until the thread id is known to the caller.\n            await db.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM dispatch_timings WHERE intent_id IN \"\n                \"(SELECT id FROM intents WHERE session_id = ?)\",\n                (session_id,),\n            )\n            await db.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM intents WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n            )\n            await db.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM utterances WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n            )\n            await db.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM topic_context_cache WHERE topic_id IN \"\n                \"(SELECT id FROM topics WHERE session_id = ?)\",\n                (session_id,),\n            )\n            await db.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM topics WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n            )\n            await db.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM surfaces WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n            )\n            await db.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM sessions WHERE id = ?\", (session_id,)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n        return {\"session_removed\": session_removed, \"topics_removed\": topics_removed}\n\n    # Surface operations\n    async def register_surface(\n        self,\n        session_id: str,\n        surface_type: str,\n        always_available: bool = False,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Register a new surface for a session.\"\"\"\n        surface_id = str(uuid4())\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"\"\"INSERT INTO surfaces\n                   (id, session_id, type, state, always_available, last_seen)\n                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, 'active', ?, ?)\"\"\",\n                (surface_id, session_id, surface_type, 1 if always_available else 0, now)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n        return surface_id\n\n    async def update_surface_heartbeat(self, surface_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Update surface last_seen timestamp.\"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"UPDATE surfaces SET last_seen = ?, state = 'active' WHERE id = ?\",\n                (now, surface_id)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def mark_surface_disconnected(self, surface_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Mark a surface as disconnected.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"UPDATE surfaces SET state = 'disconnected' WHERE id = ?\",\n                (surface_id,)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def get_active_surfaces(self, session_id: str) -> list[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get all active surfaces for a session, ordered by last_seen.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT * FROM surfaces\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND state IN ('active', 'idle')\n                   ORDER BY last_seen DESC\"\"\",\n                (session_id,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n                return [dict(row) for row in rows]\n\n    async def get_fallback_surface(self) -> Optional[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get the always-available fallback surface (Telegram).\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT * FROM surfaces\n                   WHERE always_available = 1 AND state != 'disconnected'\n                   LIMIT 1\"\"\"\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                return dict(row) if row else None\n\n    # Utterance operations\n    async def create_utterance(self, session_id: str, raw_text: str, utterance_id: str | None = None) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Create a new utterance and return its ID.\"\"\"\n        utterance_id = utterance_id or str(uuid4())\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"INSERT INTO utterances (id, session_id, raw_text, created_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\",\n                (utterance_id, session_id, raw_text, now)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n        return utterance_id\n\n    # Intent operations\n    async def create_intent(\n        self,\n        utterance_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        project_slug: str | None,\n        intent_type: str,\n        bead_ref: str | None = None,\n        lookup_kind: str | None = None,\n        topic_id: str | None = None,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Create a new intent and return its ID.\"\"\"\n        intent_id = str(uuid4())\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"\"\"INSERT INTO intents\n                   (id, utterance_id, session_id, project_slug, intent_type, lookup_kind, bead_ref, topic_id, created_at)\n                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\"\",\n                (intent_id, utterance_id, session_id, project_slug, intent_type, lookup_kind, bead_ref, topic_id, now)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n        return intent_id\n\n    async def update_intent_status(\n        self,\n        intent_id: str,\n        status: str,\n        resolved_at: int | None = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Update intent status.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            if resolved_at is None and status == \"resolved\":\n                resolved_at = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n            await db.execute(\n                \"UPDATE intents SET status = ?, resolved_at = ? WHERE id = ?\",\n                (status, resolved_at, intent_id)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def get_pending_intents(self, session_id: str) -> list[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get all pending intents for a session.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT * FROM intents\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND status IN ('pending', 'dispatched')\n                   ORDER BY created_at DESC\"\"\",\n                (session_id,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n                return [dict(row) for row in rows]\n\n    async def get_intent_by_bead_ref(self, bead_ref: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get intent by bead reference where status is tracked.\n\n        Returns intent row WHERE bead_ref == <bead_ref> AND status IN\n        ('pending', 'dispatched', 'stuck'), or None if no match.\n        Used by the bead watcher to resolve closed beads to their\n        original intent context.\n        \"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT * FROM intents\n                   WHERE bead_ref = ? AND status IN ('pending', 'dispatched', 'stuck')\n                   LIMIT 1\"\"\",\n                (bead_ref,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                return dict(row) if row else None\n\n    async def get_intent(self, intent_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get intent by ID.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT * FROM intents WHERE id = ? LIMIT 1\"\"\",\n                (intent_id,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                return dict(row) if row else None\n\n    # Result operations\n    async def create_result(\n        self,\n        intent_id: str | None,\n        topic_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        summary: str,\n        data: dict,\n        urgency: str = \"normal\",\n        result_type: str | None = None,\n        previous_result_id: str | None = None,\n        diff_summary: str | None = None,\n        diff_data: dict | None = None,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Create a new result and return its ID.\n\n        Args:\n            intent_id: Intent thread ID (None for monitoring-originated results)\n            topic_id: Topic ID\n            session_id: Session ID\n            summary: Result summary\n            data: Result data (dict)\n            urgency: Urgency level\n            result_type: Result type for component selection (e.g., 'monitoring:{project_slug}')\n            previous_result_id: Previous result ID for diff\n            diff_summary: Human-readable diff summary\n            diff_data: Detailed diff data\n        \"\"\"\n        import json\n        result_id = str(uuid4())\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"\"\"INSERT INTO results\n                   (id, intent_id, topic_id, session_id, summary, data, urgency, result_type, created_at, surfaced_at,\n                    previous_result_id, diff_summary, diff_data)\n                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\"\",\n                (\n                    result_id, intent_id, topic_id, session_id, summary, json.dumps(data),\n                    urgency, result_type, now, now, previous_result_id, diff_summary,\n                    json.dumps(diff_data) if diff_data else None\n                )\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n        return result_id\n\n    async def get_unsurfed_results(self, session_id: str) -> list[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get results that haven't been surfaced to the user yet.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT * FROM results\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND surfaced_at IS NULL\n                   ORDER BY created_at ASC\"\"\",\n                (session_id,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n                return [dict(row) for row in rows]\n\n    async def mark_results_surfed(self, session_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Mark all unsurfed results as surfaced.\"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"UPDATE results SET surfaced_at = ? WHERE session_id = ? AND surfaced_at IS NULL\",\n                (now, session_id)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def mark_results_surfed_by_ids(self, session_id: str, result_ids: list[str]) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Mark specific results as surfaced.\"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            for result_id in result_ids:\n                await db.execute(\n                    \"UPDATE results SET surfaced_at = ? WHERE id = ? AND session_id = ?\",\n                    (now, result_id, session_id)\n                )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def get_results_for_intent(self, intent_id: str) -> list[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get all results for a specific intent.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT * FROM results\n                   WHERE intent_id = ?\n                   ORDER BY created_at DESC\"\"\",\n                (intent_id,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n                return [dict(row) for row in rows]\n\n    async def get_all_results(self) -> list[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get all results in the database.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT * FROM results ORDER BY created_at DESC\"\"\"\n            ) as cursor:\n                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n                return [dict(row) for row in rows]\n\n    # Topic operations\n    async def create_topic(\n        self,\n        label: str,\n        topic_type: str = \"adhoc\",\n        project_slugs: list[str] | None = None,\n        scope: str = \"session\",\n        session_id: str | None = None,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Create a new topic and return its ID.\"\"\"\n        import json\n        topic_id = str(uuid4())\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"\"\"INSERT INTO topics\n                   (id, label, type, project_slugs, scope, session_id, created_at, last_active)\n                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\"\",\n                (topic_id, label, topic_type, json.dumps(project_slugs or []), scope, session_id, now, now)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n        return topic_id\n\n    async def find_or_create_topic(\n        self,\n        label: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        topic_type: str = \"adhoc\",\n        project_slugs: list[str] | None = None,\n    ) -> tuple[str, bool]:\n        \"\"\"Find existing topic by label and session, or create new. Returns (topic_id, created).\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            # Try to find existing topic in session scope\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT id FROM topics\n                   WHERE label = ? AND scope = 'session' AND session_id = ? AND archived_at IS NULL\n                   LIMIT 1\"\"\",\n                (label, session_id)\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                if row:\n                    return row[0], False\n\n        # Create new topic\n        topic_id = await self.create_topic(label, topic_type, project_slugs, \"session\", session_id)\n        return topic_id, True\n\n    async def update_topic_activity(self, topic_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Update topic last_active timestamp.\"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"UPDATE topics SET last_active = ? AND status IN ('pending', 'dispatched')\",\n                (session_id,)\n            ))[0][0]\n\n            # Get new results (unsurfaced results)\n            new_results = (await db.execute_fetchall(\n                \"\"\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM results\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND surfaced_at IS NULL\"\"\",\n                (session_id,)\n            ))[0][0]\n\n            # Get exceptions (HUMAN beads or high urgency items)\n            exceptions = (await db.execute_fetchall(\n                \"\"\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM results\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND urgency IN ('critical', 'high') AND acked_at IS NULL\"\"\",\n                (session_id,)\n            ))[0][0]\n\n            return {\n                \"pending_intents\": pending_count,\n                \"new_results\": new_results,\n                \"unresolved_exceptions\": exceptions,\n            }\n\n    # Topic context cache operations\n    async def set_topic_context(\n        self,\n        topic_id: str,\n        context_data: dict,\n        ttl_seconds: int = 600,  # Default 10 minutes\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Cache pre-fetched context for a topic.\"\"\"\n        import json\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        expires_at = now + ttl_seconds\n\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"\"\"INSERT OR REPLACE INTO topic_context_cache\n                   (topic_id, context_data, fetched_at, expires_at)\n                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\"\"\",\n                (topic_id, json.dumps(context_data), now, expires_at)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def get_topic_context(self, topic_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get cached context for a topic if still valid.\"\"\"\n        import json\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT context_data, expires_at FROM topic_context_cache\n                   WHERE topic_id = ? AND expires_at > ?\"\"\",\n                (topic_id, now)\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                if row:\n                    return {\n                        \"context\": json.loads(row[\"context_data\"]),\n                        \"expires_at\": row[\"expires_at\"],\n                    }\n        return None\n\n    async def invalidate_topic_context(self, topic_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Invalidate cached context for a topic.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM topic_context_cache WHERE topic_id = ?\",\n                (topic_id,)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def cleanup_expired_context(self) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Remove expired context cache entries. Ensure the row exists (created_at set once, on first write).\n            await db.execute(\n                \"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO dispatch_timings (intent_id, created_at) VALUES (?, ?)\",\n                (intent_id, now),\n            )\n            if cols:\n                set_clause = \", \".join(f\"{c} = ?\" for c in cols)\n                params = [*cols.values(), intent_id]\n                await db.execute(\n                    f\"UPDATE dispatch_timings SET {set_clause} WHERE intent_id = ?\",\n                    params,\n                )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def get_dispatch_timings(self, intent_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get the dispatch_timings row for one intent thread, or None.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"SELECT * FROM dispatch_timings WHERE intent_id = ?\",\n                (intent_id,),\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                return dict(row) if row else None\n\n    async def get_latency_percentiles(self, since: int | None = None) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Aggregate p50/p95 per stage across dispatch_timings rows.\n\n        Returns ``{stage: {\"p50\": ms, \"p95\": ms, \"count\": n}}`` for every stage\n        that has at least one non-NULL value. NULLs are skipped per stage (so a\n        stage not yet captured for any dispatch simply doesn't appear). Pass\n        ``since`` (a unix timestamp) to window to recent dispatches only — the\n        latency-baseline bead consumes the un-windowed call to fill the plan's\n        global Measured p50/p95 columns.\n\n        Percentiles are nearest-rank (ceil(q/100 * n)), computed in Python\n        because SQLite has no built-in percentile.\n        \"\"\"\n        from ..instrument.timings import percentiles\n\n        result: dict[str, dict] = {}\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            for stage in DISPATCH_TIMING_COLUMNS:\n                if since is not None:\n                    query = (\n                        f\"SELECT {stage} FROM dispatch_timings \"\n                        f\"WHERE {stage} IS NOT NULL AND created_at >= ? \"\n                        f\"ORDER BY {stage}\"\n                    )\n                    params: tuple = (since,)\n                else:\n                    query = (\n                        f\"SELECT {stage} FROM dispatch_timings \"\n                        f\"WHERE {stage} IS NOT NULL ORDER BY {stage}\"\n                    )\n                    params = ()\n                async with db.execute(query, params) as cursor:\n                    values = [row[0] for row in await cursor.fetchall()]\n                if not values:\n                    continue\n                pct = percentiles(values, qs=(50, 95))\n                result[stage] = {\n                    \"p50\": pct[50],\n                    \"p95\": pct[95],\n                    \"count\": len(values),\n                }\n        return result\n\n    async def get_session_feedback_summary(\n        self,\n        session_id: str,\n        since: int | None = None,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Get a summary of feedback signals for a session.\"\"\"\n        one_hour_ago = int(datetime.now().timestamp()) - 3600\n\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n\n            # Get signal counts by type\n            since_clause = since or one_hour_ago\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT signal_type, COUNT(*) as count\n                   FROM feedback_signals\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND timestamp >= ?\n                   GROUP BY signal_type\"\"\",\n                (session_id, since_clause)\n            ) as cursor:\n                type_counts = {row[\"signal_type\"]: row[\"count\"] for row in await cursor.fetchall()}\n\n            # Get average ack delay\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT AVG(CAST(data->>'ack_delay_seconds' AS INTEGER)) as avg_delay\n                   FROM feedback_signals\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND signal_type = 'ack_speed' AND timestamp >= ?\"\"\",\n                (session_id, since_clause)\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                avg_ack_delay = row[\"avg_delay\"] if row and row[\"avg_delay\"] else 0\n\n            return {\n                \"signal_counts\": type_counts,\n                \"avg_ack_delay_seconds\": avg_ack_delay,\n            }\n\n    async def create_result_with_diff(\n        self,\n        intent_id: str,\n        topic_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        summary: str,\n        data: dict,\n        urgency: str = \"normal\",\n    ) -> tuple[str, bool]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Create a new result with automatic diff computation.\n\n        Returns (result_id, has_diff).\n\n        Convenience method that automatically computes the diff against\n        the previous result for the same topic (if one exists).\n        \"\"\"\n        from ..diff.engine import get_diff_engine\n\n        # Get previous result for this topic\n        previous_result = await self.get_latest_result_for_topic(topic_id)\n\n        # Compute diff if we have a previous result\n        diff_summary = None\n        diff_data = None\n        has_diff = False\n\n        if previous_result:\n            diff_engine = get_diff_engine()\n            diff_result = await diff_engine.compute_diff(\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                previous_result=previous_result,\n                current_result={\"data\": data},\n            )\n\n            if diff_result.has_changes:\n                has_diff = True\n                diff_summary = diff_result.change_summary\n                diff_data = {\n                    \"fields\": [\n                        {\n                            \"field_name\": f.field_name,\n                            \"old_value\": f.old_value,\n                            \"new_value\": f.new_value,\n                            \"change_type\": f.change_type,\n                        }\n                        for f in diff_result.fields\n                    ],\n                    \"summary\": diff_result.summary,\n                }\n\n        # Create the result with diff data\n        result_id = await self.create_result(\n            intent_id=intent_id,\n            topic_id=topic_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            summary=summary,\n            data=data,\n            urgency=urgency,\n            previous_result_id=previous_result[\"id\"] if previous_result else None,\n            diff_summary=diff_summary,\n            diff_data=diff_data,\n        )\n\n        return result_id, has_diff\n\n    # {"type":"stream_event","event":{"type":"message_stop"},"session_id":"f0f3f298-e1a4-40ce-8542-0a154e76907e","parent_tool_use_id":null,"uuid":"5dbc4999-7665-4d74-8e5e-7fd16e0b55f8"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_a9ad8074a46a4115b60bf2d0","type":"tool_result","content":"The diagram above is the happy path only. Beads also get refused, fail repeatedly, or sit unclaimed — and NEEDLE has no built-in circuit breaker: it re-dispatches a failing bead forever. The async path therefore also specifies:\n\n- **Re-dispatch circuit breaker.** NEEDLE exposes no dispatch-attempt API, so the breaker's signal is the bead's own comment stream: a worker that declines a bead appends a structured refusal comment — `bf comment <id> \"REFUSED: <reason>\"` — the same convention the live incident's workers followed when they committed refusal records to the bead. Each tick the watcher checks its open tracked beads (every unresolved `intents.bead_ref`) alongside the closed list, parses comments past a per-bead high-water mark, and persists the running refusal count in the watcher-owned `bead_watch` table (see Data Model) — on disk, so breaker state survives watcher restarts. After N refusals (default 3) or T hours without progress (default 24h — no closure and no new non-refusal comment), it fences the bead — `bf update` to `status=blocked`, so workers stop picking it up — sets the intent's status to `stuck`, and pushes a \"task stuck — needs your input\" card to the active surface with the most recent refusal reason attached.\n- **Terminal failures.** A bead closed as failed/refused resolves its intent to `failed`; the reason surfaces as a card, never silently dropped. User cancellation still maps to `cancelled`.\n- **Visible aging.** Pending cards show elapsed time; a card past its SLA is flagged on canvas before the breaker trips, so a stuck task is never invisible. The SLA is defined, not vibes: built-in per-intent-type defaults (task-profile: 6h; hot-path intents flag at 30s pending, since their budget is 3s) with a per-project `sla_hours` override in the registry entry. Flag ownership is split by path: for bead-backed (task-profile) intents the watcher computes `sla_deadline` at bead creation, stores it in `bead_watch`, and stamps `sla_flagged_at` when the flag fires. Hot-path intents have no bead and no watcher row — their 30s flag is owned by the **canvas client**: a pure client-side timer from the local pending placeholder's creation at submit time (the canvas creates the placeholder before any server response — see Escalate Strand, Pending/ack card render path) applies the aged-pending treatment with no server round-trip, deliberately, because a hung or wedged server is exactly the failure this flag must survive (see the matching Degraded-State UX row).\n\n### The Self-Improvement Path\n\n```\nFeedback signal (explicit instruction or implicit engagement pattern)\n--\n    description: \"Options data pipeline on apexalgo-iad\"\n    cluster: apexalgo-iad  # ArgoCD endpoint for this cluster resolves via config/clusters.yaml (see Fetch Strand — Cluster→ArgoCD Endpoint Resolution)\n    namespace: options\n    repo_path: /home/coding/options-pipeline  # local checkout: git-log + per-project bead listing\n    argocd_app: options-pipeline  # ArgoCD Application name for the fetch matrix's sync row; defaults to the project slug when omitted\n    sla_hours: 6          # pending-card SLA override (see The Async Path — Visible aging)\n    intent_support: [status, action, brainstorm, task-profile]\n    # `action` appears in intent_support for routing completeness, but action\n    # dispatch is DISABLED until the Action strand ships — see Action Execution\n    # Model (Status: executor NOT BUILT)\n    workflows:\n--\n3. Writes result to `results` table and marks the intent `resolved` (or `failed`, per The Async Path's terminal-failure rule)\n4. Fires SSE push to canvas or Telegram push if no canvas active\n\n> ⚠ The Telegram push in step 4 is NON-FUNCTIONAL until ADR-1 lands — `src/telegram/fallback.py`'s delivery methods are stubs pointed at an unreachable bridge URL (see ADR-1). Today, a bead closing with no active canvas writes to `results` but notifies no one.\n\nThe same poll loop drives the async-path circuit breaker (see The Async Path): each tick it folds newly parsed refusal comments into `bead_watch` (refusal count, last reason, comment high-water mark), flags cards whose `sla_deadline` has passed, and fences stuck beads to `status=blocked`, flipping their intents to `stuck`. Breaker and SLA state live in `bead_watch` in the session store — never only in memory — so a watcher restart loses nothing.\n\n**Ambient monitoring tick.** The watcher daemon is also the runtime for ambient monitoring — there is no separate monitoring process. On its own timer (`tick_interval_seconds` in `config/monitoring.yaml`, default 300; the config hot-reloads per tick per the Hot-Reload Architecture), the daemon evaluates each rule in `config/monitoring.yaml` against its watched topic: `src/monitoring/ambient.py` runs the relevant fetch-matrix sources through the fetch strand, diffs the output against `topic_context_cache`, and when a rule fires writes a `results` row directly — `topic_id` set, `intent_id` NULL (system-originated, no utterance behind it), `urgency` and exception type taken from the rule via `config/monitoring.yaml` and `config/exceptions.yaml`, `summary` filled from the rule's deterministic template (no LLM anywhere on the tick). From there the result enters Surface Routing Rules like any other: SSE push to the active surface, exception-class handling when urgency is critical.\n\nPure I/O. Not a separately started process: FastAPI's lifespan startup spawns the watcher loop in-process, so the single Stage A run command brings up hot path, watcher, ambient monitoring, and circuit breaker together — the server cannot be up with the watcher silently absent. Liveness is verified, not assumed: the watcher stamps `last_tick_at` after every poll tick, and `GET /health` exposes a `watcher` block (`alive`, `last_tick_at`, tick count) where `alive` is true only while the task is running **and** `last_tick_at` is within 2× the poll interval. A crashed watcher task is logged and restarted with backoff by the lifespan supervisor; the `/health` gap makes the crash observable. Under Deploy Stage B the same in-process model ships inside the aide-de-camp Deployment (never a K8s Job/CronJob, per infrastructure conventions).\n\n--\n  intent_id TEXT,\n  topic_id  TEXT,\n  PRIMARY KEY (intent_id, topic_id)\n)","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"f0f3f298-e1a4-40ce-8542-0a154e76907e","uuid":"1b3c5c7b-5a6a-440b-ae72-8ff6ba22eed8","timestamp":"2026-07-23T05:29:58.973Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"The diagram above is the happy path only.
